weaknesses: Only one season of starting experience at the FCS level.
Needs a makeover with his passing mechanics.
Throws with a wide base and leaves his back half behind in delivery.
Unable to generate NFL-caliber drive throws into tight windows.
Not prepared to dissect zone coverage into intermediate spaces.
Likely requires a tailored scheme to fit his strengths/weaknesses.

strengths: Triggers into the alley, attacking blocks and runners with force.
Plays and tackles with enough physicality near the box.
Has the size and strength to match up against tight ends.
Adequate vision and break quickness from zone coverages.
weaknesses: Carries short arms and below-average wingspan.
Clunky backpedal with below-average fluidity in transitions.
Lacks burst/agility needed to make up for bad pursuit angles.
Missed time in 2022 (Achilles injury) and all of 2024 season (foot surgery).

strengths: Three-year starter and team captain in 2025.
Has experience playing outside and inside at nickelback.
Displayed improved patience in reading routes from off-man.
Communicates well when passing receivers off in zone.
Adequate eye balance to adjust coverage to route combinations.
Balances high-low route responsibilities in zone.
weaknesses: Lacks coordination and foot quickness to successfully press.
Infrequent to locate the ball with his back to the passer.
Doesn’t run well enough to make up distance when beaten.
Below-average hips to transition smoothly with route breaks.
Might not have the burst or short-area quickness to find NFL ball production.
Steep pursuit angles cause him to lose contain positioning.

strengths: Plays with box-safety physicality in coverage and run support.
Punches and suffocates outside release against boundary.
Demands the catch space on jump-ball battles.
Gathers and triggers with adequate fluidity from depth.
Operates with discipline/leverage against high/low route concepts.
Explodes vertically with extended arms to capture high-point.
Run-defense wiring is exactly what coaches want to see.
Above-average stopping power when tackling bigger backs.
weaknesses: Could struggle matching twitchy NFL releases.
Hips can get sticky on sudden, aggressive transitions.
Grabby at break points or vertical routes when he’s out of phase.
Gets too dialed in to receiver and loses track of ball flight.
Still developing his instincts from off coverages.
Leggy with sluggish change of direction as open-field tackler.

strengths: Should continue filling out his frame.
Good snap/reach and snap/step quickness.
Strong hands and core to neutralize/seal at the point.
Athletic but patient when climbing up to linebackers.
Plays with desired physicality and finish as a run blocker.
Identifies rush front and sets protections.
Consistent in re-leveraging his hands when needed.
Wrestling background shows up with hip roll to anchor.
weaknesses: Early draft entry and 2025 ACL injury limited his career snap count.
Lacks leg drive to uproot and turn an odd-front nose tackle.
Average footwork to hit landmarks in wide zone.
Needs to play with lighter feet in pass protection.
Edges become leaky against rush quickness.
Oversets open pathways for inside counters.

strengths: Four-year starter.
Impressive get-off/footwork to reach and seal in wide zone.
Has ability to adjust on second-level targets.
Quick snap-to-punch hand quickness.
Lands punch with extension and latches hands in.
weaknesses: Frame is lacking functional mass.
Plays with high pads and poor leverage at the point.
Discarded quickly by powerful nose tackles.
Lacks plays strength to press pass rushers off of him.
Inconsistent footwork and set depth defending twists.

strengths: Well-built and showed blazing speed at his pro day.
Averaged 72 catches and 1,043 yards in the last two seasons.
Works back to the throw on curls and comebacks.
Utilizes size and strength to stack corners on go routes.
Wins vertically with pure speed or double moves.
Gives enough effort as a blocker to help in the run game.
weaknesses: Struggled badly to elude or separate against Arkansas.
Below-average deceleration and footwork in shorter spaces.
Unable to secure contested catches at an adequate rate.
Concerning drop rate has followed him throughout his career.
Missed all but two games in 2022 with foot injury that required surgery.

strengths: Puts immediate pressure on defense with burst off the line.
Short-route tempo bolsters separation.
Excellent top-end speed to separate on go routes and slot fades.
Was a consistent winner in one-on-one drills at the Senior Bowl.
Quick stick-and-stop on stop routes and quickly transitions from catch to run.
Provides run-game support with his run-after-catch talent on slot screens.
Slips tacklers in tight quarters with jitterbug quickness.
weaknesses: Will need to prove himself against tight press looks.
Rounded routes put quarterback’s throws in harm’s way.
Inconsistent balance getting out of sharp breaks.
Gets pushed around when sharing the catch space.
Average path adjustments when tracking the deep ball.

strengths: Plays zone with excellent eye discipline, route leverage and spacing.
Speed to stay in-phase vertically and rarely loses position once he gains it.
Outstanding ball-tracker with the ability to irritate downfield catch points.
Diagnoses run, slips blocks and quickly gets to ball-carriers.
Consistently finishes tackles against the run and pass.
Used impressive acceleration to block two punts while at Indiana.
Opened playoff blowout of Oregon with a pick-six on the first play from scrimmage.
weaknesses: Struggles to flip hips cleanly when route leverage is beaten.
Loses ground to physical receivers at the top of the route.
Average plant-and-drive quickness from top of his drop.
Lack of length will cost him pass breakups at times.
Can be engulfed by big-bodied blockers near the line.
Drag-down tackler in space against bigger wideouts.

strengths: Outstanding durability as a three-year starter.
Lacks ideal height but long arms make up for it.
Bends and enters first contact with good leverage.
Works feet and hips into position to seal his block.
Core strength and quick hand resets stabilize base blocks.
Able to step on the brakes when bull rush sees early life.
Plays with quick eyes and is poised in the face of rush games.
weaknesses: Comes forward with helmet first and excessive lean.
Lacks body control and timing on combo blocks.
Success level diminishes when asked to block in space.
Rarely cinches up and controls rusher with grip strength.
Protection could use better posture and base width.
Tight hips limit his ability to recover when beaten.

strengths: Rarely used vertically but appears to have adequate speed.
Controlled footwork to stutter and break at the top of the route.
Uses hips to partition off coverage from the catch-point.
Showed off better movement talent at Senior Bowl practices than he did on tape.
Brings in tall, off-frame throws with his 6-foot-9 wingspan.
Leg strength makes him tougher to bring down after the catch.
Had some success sealing the edge in run game and mirroring pass rushers.
weaknesses: Only one season as a starter in college.
Upright through routes and telegraphs what is coming next.
Needs to sink, open and present to quarterback with better suddenness.
Inconsistent knee bend and reactive mirroring as blocker in space.
Hands can come in a little too wide in his base blocks.

strengths: Accelerates quickly off the snap.
Has top-end speed to maintain a lead once he’s in position.
Runs routes with good play speed and urgent pace.
Flashes impressive competitiveness to make catches in tight traffic.
Viable punt-return option with short-area elusiveness.
Showed great explosiveness in his pro day athletic testing.
weaknesses: Falls well below the size standards for some NFL teams.
Can be lackadaisical instead of aggressively conning coverage in his routes.
Would benefit from avoiding so much contact at the top of the route.
Needs to tighten route turns to close off undercutting angles for defenders.
Body-catcher who suffers from focus drops.

strengths: Hits man coverage with tempo changes to create space.
Builds speed to threatening levels down the seam.
Manipulates coverage with aggressive fakes at the stem.
Instinctive in finding openings and opportunities in space.
Locates safety and adjusts accordingly when working downfield.
Searches for yards after catch and will find them.
Adjusts in space and lands as a move blocker.
weaknesses: Doesn’t fight hard enough to clear clingy coverage.
Clunky getting in and out of intermediate breaks.
Inconsistent use of frame to box out defenders.
Labors adjusting to low and back-hip throws.
Run-blocking technique at the point is below average.
Needs to become more consistently competitive as a blocker.

strengths: Big target with long wingspan.
Adequate timing to the linebacker on work-up blocks.
Can cover and stay engaged with second-level targets.
Extends and adjusts to retrieve high throws.
Above-average focus to bring passes in.
Hands are soft but sticky with few unforced drops.
weaknesses: Needs to work with quicker, inside hands as a run blocker.
Inconsistent fitting his run blocks with proper positioning.
Below-average drive through contact in his lower half.
Plays with a lack of tenacity to sustain blocks for long.
Allows closing defenders to eat off his plate when contested.
Can be a lumbering route-runner in space.

strengths: Loves to make tackles, piling up 277 of them since 2023.
Fills run fits as down safety with a quick trigger.
Appears to have coverage chops to slide into big nickel when needed.
Backpedal and transitions are smooth in space.
Properly balanced in reading quarterback and route development.
Instincts and recognition keep him dialed into coverage duties.
Wired and built for production on punt/kick cover teams.
weaknesses: Height, weight, length fall below NFL standards.
Feels a little small playing inside the box.
Struggles to avoid or play off of climbing blocks.
Slips in coverage when over-extended beyond his base.
Will struggle guarding vertical speed and big tight ends.
Tackle slippage derived from his lack of wrap-up length.

strengths: Possesses elite arm length and athleticism for his size.
Drops hips and explodes under his hands into drive blocks.
Body control and agility allow him to adjust to movement in space.
Has the foot quickness to feature more jump-sets as a pro.
Can sap a rusher's confidence when punch is timed up.
Keeps head tucked and back flat during protection reps.
Resets hands to reassert length after falling behind.
weaknesses: Technique is still a work in progress.
Operates with tall pad level when fitting up run blocks.
Secure block needs better thump before working up on combos.
Hands frequently land high and/or outside the frame.
Elongated pass slides create openings for inside counters.
Lacks feel for pocket depth and set points.
Footwork and base width become sloppy when redirecting weight.

strengths: Unusually long for his height with a wide wingspan.
Frame stays clean with well-placed punch and easy arm extension.
Quality hand work allows him to navigate around the block.
Able to post up and peek in on backfield when two-gapping.
Displays twitchy upper body to shed block on demand.
Impressive contact balance keeps him off the ground.
weaknesses: Gets beat to the spot and sealed by lateral blocks.
Needs better feel and take-on plan against doubles teams.
Inconsistent keeping pads square through engagement.
Lacks plan and production as a pass rusher.
Below-average bend and agility to capture moving quarterbacks.

strengths: Dynamic skill set with talent to catch, run and pass.
Alters speed to sneak separation at break points.
Tough in traffic with strong hands to secure combat catches.
Can operate zone-read and throw out of that look.
Rarely stopped on short-yardage carries.
Determined and willful once the ball is in his hands.
Shows some strain when connected to a block.
weaknesses: Behind the curve with receiving fundamentals.
Still learning to generate leverage as a route-runner.
Needs to play with quicker sink-and-show on stop routes.
Allows clingy coverage too much leeway into his catch space.
Requires more work in the weight room as a blocker.
Needs to play with inside hands and better placement.

strengths: Shows attention to detail in his route-running.
Gets out of his breaks at crisp angles to create space.
Catches the point of the football with soft, sticky hands.
Makes grabs in heavy traffic without blinking.
Possesses excellent awareness of the sideline.
weaknesses: Play strength doesn’t appear to match his listed size.
Missing the foot quickness to quickly skip and slide past press.
Struggles to ward off physical man coverage.
Unable to open NFL catch windows with burst out of breaks.
Lack of vertical speed and second gear shows up on tape.

strengths: Heavy hands jolt tackles and create space to operate in both phases.
Uses up-and-over arm action to clear the inside edge.
Smooth inside spin opens rush-lane opportunities.
Can slide laterally with zone blocks and redirect through them against cutbacks.
Displays powerful pull-and-shed technique at the point.
Takes on heavy contact but stays in the fight.
weaknesses: Plodding upfield rush lands down the middle of the tackle.
Limited bend to flatten and shrink the pocket.
Slow to counter when rush stalls at the top.
Plays with elevated pad level throughout the rep.
Lacks tackle range and can be wiggled into misses by runners.
Needs discipline to cut down roughing penalties.

strengths: Girthy frame with drive power to displace defenders.
Gets into short pulls with adequate footwork.
Pass protects with good base width and square pads.
Displays adequate recognition of rush games up front.
Length/strength to redirect defenders who find his edge.
Unlocks lower half to sink a firm anchor when needed.
weaknesses: Below-average short-area movement limits his range.
Struggles getting to cut-off blocks and second-level landmarks.
Fairly average thump on initial contact for his size.
Average punch quickness and hand usage in pass pro.
Below-average slide quickness to seal gaps against athletic rushers.

strengths: Averaged 77 catches for 1,060 yards and 12.7 TDs over the past three seasons.
Plus early acceleration and short-area quickness.
Can stick a foot in the ground and break sharply for complex routes.
Above-average execution of his double moves.
Pummelled opposition with fade-route wins near the goal line.
Above-average body control when elevating to catch.
weaknesses: Showed below-average speed at his pro day.
Too upright getting into his route breaks.
Focus drops pop up each year on tape.
Could struggle against press corners.
Lacks separation needed to play outside.

strengths: Enormous presence in the middle with proportional build.
Giant hands at the end of long arms spanning a 7-foot-2 wingspan.
When he attacks blocks properly, he can consume two gaps.
Can reach out and slow runners with an arm even when he’s being blocked.
Uses his length and leg drive to crank up bull rush on centers.
Moves well enough to bring down the quarterback once he’s in the pocket.
weaknesses: Plays with tight hips and a narrow base relative to his frame.
Needs quicker hands and better upper-body power to control blockers.
Blockers can crowd his frame and uproot his anchor to clear him out.
Below-average core power and contact balance for power swaps.
Movement is mechanical and lacking athletic flow.
Hasn’t learned to weaponize his length as a rusher.

strengths: Possesses adequate wingspan and good hand size.
Plays with solid upper-body power.
Good reaction quickness to lateral movement off the snap.
Quick to press and separate from blocks at the point of attack.
Locates ball-carriers and works off blocks toward the action.
weaknesses: Only one season of plus production on FBS level.
Below-average mass for duties at nose tackle.
Gets pushed around by double-teams and down blocks.
Allows pad level to rise during some engagements.
Doesn’t have opening moves to unlock his pass rush.

strengths: Girthy through hips, thighs and calves.
Displays power to punch and steer blocks aside.
Plays with consistent force and effort on each snap.
Fights to beat cut-offs and maintain gap integrity against wide zone.
Plays with the same force and effort when fatigued.
Violent clubs, slaps and rips are part of his rush package.
weaknesses: Career production underwhelmed.
Lacks length to keep big bodies from crowding into his frame.
Puts up a fight, but lacks true gap-control technique.
Base narrows as a move rusher, leading to balance losses.
Short levers make it harder to flip the edge and find the pocket.

strengths: Carries imposing frame with long arms.
Can delay press release with heavy hands.
Chokes off landing space on go balls down the sideline.
When he’s in position, few catches will be finished.
Better technique could unlock a higher ceiling.
Ready to fill or set edges depending on what is needed.
Proactively looks for chances to support the run.
weaknesses: Finished career with a jaw-dropping 30 penalties, per PFF.
Reaches and grabs when route adversity strikes.
Lacks fluidity in hips and feet to stay sticky in man.
Struggles to recover cleanly when leverage is beaten.
Still waiting for him to turn and find the football in flight.
More shoulder strikes than wrap-ups in run support.

strengths: Possesses the speed to bounce runs and compete for the corner.
Good vision and decision-making as an interior runner.
Shows patience and timing in juking open-field tacklers.
Adequate pass-catching option out of the backfield.
Stout in blitz pickup.
weaknesses: Size falls below average.
Hip tightness prevents sudden, sharp lateral cuts.
Struggles to re-accelerate after slowing his feet.
Lacks explosiveness into and through contact to fall forward.

strengths: Sturdy, thick boundary cornerback with good length.
Stays square and waits for release declaration from press.
Effective reading and jumping routes from short zones.
Agile feet create efficient footwork in pattern matching.
Anticipates breaks and blankets comebacks from tight man.
Had interceptions in each of the last two SEC title games.
Presses the line and sets the edge when uncovered.
weaknesses: Allows too many easy pitch-and-catch throws in front of him.
Average acceleration out of transitions.
Lacks desired recovery speed when beaten.
Grabs and holds become easy calls.
Needs better breakdown and control as an open-field tackler.
Drag-down tackler with consistently high entry points.

strengths: Good initial acceleration into his routes.
Pays attention to break routes off at crisp angles.
Smooth late adjustments to grab advantages downfield.
Able to meet throws with good hand extension.
Builds speed quickly after catch for chunk plays.
Fits into block targets and fights to stick around.
weaknesses: Linear route movements lack unpredictable elements.
Leggy with a lack of separation burst from breaks underneath.
Average hip sink getting into intermediate cuts.
Needs to play with greater fight when contested.
Inconsistent finishing his catches through contact.

strengths: Thick-bodied with good length.
Strikes quickly from leveraged position with heavy hands.
Lifts, presses and muscles through blockers.
Surprising first step laterally to match zone-block steps.
Eats blocks and maintains footing with plus contact balance.
Has foot quickness for activation in rush games.
Able to generate some pocket push when he’s singled up.
weaknesses: Wins with will over technique at the point.
Needs better feel for angle blocks on his edge.
Lacks hand usage and openers as a pass rusher.
Rarely gets to a workable counter off his bull rush.
Comes in too high and slides off of tackle tries.

strengths: Teammates voted him a single-digit jersey – signifying one of the team’s 10 toughest players – for three consecutive years.
Assignment-oriented with discipline to find his coverage.
Recovers nicely when cross-faced by in-breaking routes.
Adequate field vision and communication from zone.
Slows the run game as an add-on defender in the box.
Length allows him to make pursuit tackles from a distance.
weaknesses: Lacks man-cover tools against wideouts.
Average burst to close from zone perch.
Below-average range to play over the top.
Comes in way too hot as a downhill run supporter.
Off-balance as an open-field tackler.

strengths: Showed good combination of speed and leaping ability at pro day.
Makes first- and second-level cuts without losing speed.
Able to hit his top speed quickly in the open field.
Keeps runs tight off the hips of blockers to maximize space.
Small catch radius but makes impressive adjustments to off-frame throws.
weaknesses: Lacks suddenness in the early stages of the run.
Will dance and wait in search of clear points of entry.
Average wiggle in short spaces to elude defenders.
Takes time to hit it north/south on swings and screens.
Lacks functional anchor to block blitzing linebackers.

strengths: Shaves in-breaking routes with disciplined, tight cuts.
Loose hips allow for smoother adjustments to back-hip throws.
Displayed vast improvement to finish combat catches.
Squares numbers to quarterback and frames his catch point.
Plays with pro footwork, dropping both feet near the boundary.
Spatial awareness present in route breaks and zone landings.
Crafty runner on jet sweeps and after the catch.
weaknesses: Play speed doesn’t always align with timed speed.
Lacks convincing push to sell his curls/comebacks.
Short arms put him at a disadvantage on 50/50 balls.
Early head turns shorten his drive phase on deep routes.
Waits for throws outside the numbers to come to him instead of attacking them.

strengths: Muscular physique with good lean mass.
Athletic with ability to find cut-off positioning as a zone blocker.
Pass slides with quick, staccato steps that stay low to the ground.
Displays vision and awareness for clean pass-offs versus twists.
Explodes his hips and hands into defenders on contact.
Good pad level and lift on opponents as a drive blocker.
weaknesses: Too much lunging at rushers on his edge.
Will get beaten to the punch too often in pass pro.
Needs more mass to withstand an interior bull rush.
Difficult for him to stay wide and centered on moving blocks.
Rarely clamps hands in or brings opponent tight for control.

strengths: Displays adequate blend of patience and urgency as a runner.
Hyper-twitchy with ability to plant and cut in an instant.
Elite burst to rocket past linebackers and onto third level.
Speed to outflank tacklers wide and/or break away in the open field.
Makes linebackers pay for missed run fits.
Stacks moves, making him hard to dial in on for tacklers.
Experienced kick returner with two career touchdowns.
weaknesses: Herky-jerky running style, lacking desired tempo for wide zone.
Modest run strength and takes on heavy contact between tackles.
Doesn’t run with complete buy-in or trust of the run blocking.
Ball security became an issue in 2025.
Has stiff hands as a pass-catcher and struggles in blitz pickup.
Does too much stutter-stepping as a kick returner.

strengths: Good early acceleration and plays faster than timed speed.
Runs routes with good fluidity.
Big-bodied target who plays to his size.
Leans on defenders without sacrificing catch readiness.
Scored 20 TDs over the last two seasons, proving himself a red-zone mismatch.
Pro-caliber ball skills with elite hand strength to finish.
Extends for off-frame throws with a spider web-like catch radius.
Ball tracking and adjustment timing are top notch.
Competitive and strong with his run-after-catch ability.
weaknesses: Production dipped when the competition level rose.
Press release is linear and will give NFL CBs opportunities to get hands on him.
Flashes early burst but lacks a true second gear.
Routes lose steam when he digs his foot in on route turns.
Earned separation closes as the route progresses.
Might need formation help and stacks/motion to free his releases.

strengths: Two-time team captain.
Rapid feet to scrape quickly across the front.
Fast trigger and burst allow him to dart into gaps.
Sudden and quick-footed as a block slipper.
Flows wide and meets lead blocks with a forceful lead shoulder.
Reactive athlete who can slide and match a back’s cut decisions.
Plus range to expand his zone capabilities.
Elusive blitzer who can stress pass protection.
weaknesses: Below-average pre-snap recognition against motion and movement.
A beat late keying blockers and racing to the junction point.
Limited feel for blocking schemes leads to inefficient movement.
Below-average hand pop to stack and shed climbing blocks.
Below-average stopping power and wrap-up consistency as a tackler.

strengths: Good size and length as a rusher.
Leveraged hand strike and leg drive propel his bull rush.
Downhill momentum is hard to stop once it starts.
Gets into the top of the rush with good forward lean.
Swallows quarterbacks and runners like a crashing wave once he’s close.
Above-average football IQ and awareness of play design.
Capable of holding his ground at the point of attack.
Plays with a smooth connection between his hands and feet.
weaknesses: Occasionally lacks energy and urgency as a run defender.
Rides on blocks instead of working quickly off of them.
Average quickness to stack, shed and pursue to the sideline.
Below-average first-step quickness in his upfield rush.
Limited hand counters cause rush to sputter if he fails to gain early advantage.
Lacks knee bend and upper-body swivel while working the turn.

strengths: Averaged 140 scrimmage yards per game in 2024.
Can joystick around tacklers as a runner, receiver and returner.
Manipulates defenders into pulling blocks on pitch plays.
Short-area cut quickness and stop-start talent leads to missed tackles.
Major mismatch on linebackers when working down the seam.
Has two touchdowns on just 18 career punt returns.
weaknesses: Diminutive frame creates durability concerns.
Unlikely to see much usage between the tackles.
Looks for “better deal” instead of keeping runs on track.
Unable to play through much contact.
Sketchy decision-maker on punt returns.
Fights the ball at times when catching punts.

strengths: Three-year starter with long arms.
Technique to sink low and corkscrew lowers against double teams.
Slips off blocks and into gaps using loose upper body.
Gets into centers quickly with good first step forward.
Uses length to disengage and shove free from the block.
weaknesses: High-cut with hips perched high in his stance.
Below-average pad level and lower-body mass to anchor the point.
Frequently beaten to the spot laterally and sealed against zone blocks.
Ends up on the ground more than he should.
Tight hips limit lateral agility to excel in rush twists.

strengths: Impressive height, weight and length for an outside corner.
Imposing figure from press with length to slow release.
Takes cues quickly from passers in short zone.
Tracks and adjusts to deep balls at top speed.
Figures to be top man on high-point battles.
Pulls arms and slaps hands when near the catch point.
Good toughness as a tackler in run support.
weaknesses: Started just 17 games in his four seasons.
Too much space and not enough squeeze as a zone defender.
Upright and sluggish in his lateral transitions with break points.
Needs to improve route recognition and decisiveness in coverage.
Top-end speed is average to close out stray vertical routes.
Missed games in 2022, 2023 and 2024 due to injuries.

strengths: Held his own against Senior Bowl competition in practices.
Stabilizes his upper half to disguise route intentions.
Split low, allowing for better balance through cuts.
Keeps footwork tight getting in and out of breaks.
Catches with natural, soft hands.
weaknesses: Has a tough time playing through contact.
Rarely sinks hips for crisp stops on comebacks.
Sits and waits on throws instead of working back to them.
Lacks top-end speed to outrun NFL coverage.
Limited catch radius creates a smaller margin for error for QBs.

strengths: Supremely competitive with good football intelligence and winning makeup.
Dual-threat option who creates for his offense on land and by air.
Impressive move accuracy (rollouts/scrambles) and third-down conversion rate.
Flashes an ability to make anticipatory and timing throws.
Big hands allow him to pump, reset and work to alternate options.
Able to quicken eyes and decision-making versus pressure without panicking.
Matched Fernando Mendoza’s completion rate on intermediate throws.
Scramble threat softens coverage structure and creates throwing windows.
Productive runner in speed option, midline, zone-read and power concepts.
weaknesses: Rare to see quarterbacks with his measurables make an NFL roster.
Production and consistency dipped against quality SEC defenses.
Inconsistent patience and decision-making against split-safety looks.
Lower half lags behind, leading to sailed throws and missed placement between the numbers.
Forced to release throws with upward trajectory due to his height.
Floats deep throws, allowing defensive backs to locate them.
Too quick to exit the side door instead of sliding and climbing.
Average open-field speed and elusiveness.

strengths: Brings energy from hips to hands with first contact.
Initial knock-back power widens the edge.
Heavy hands help wash down angle blocks.
Pairs a heavy punch with quick resets.
Locks out his length and keeps his head back.
Uses grip strength to control the rush.
Aware of twist development and remains calm.
weaknesses: Below-average body control as a run blocker.
Lack of leverage limits double-team movement.
Defenders play across his face in the run game.
Occasional struggles matching edge speed through the turn.
Needs to play with efficient use of hands in protection.
Opens post leg to an inside move instead of sliding to cut it off.

strengths: Well-built receiver with the frame of a big running back.
Highly successful weapon on jet-sweeps and reverses.
Breaks through open-field tacklers once he gets rolling downhill.
Accelerates late in route to create space.
Physical through contact in the route and at the catch-point.
Run-block potential on perimeter and when cracking down.
Has the experience and talent to become a plus kick returner.
weaknesses: Pedestrian catch production over 56 games at Georgia.
Routes lack crisp cuts and attention to detail.
Doesn’t often adjust route to avoid redirection.
Unable to pull the brakes firmly for comeback routes.
Body-catcher with below-average catch focus.
Struggles to bring in throws outside his frame.

strengths: One of the more athletic, natural movers you will find at guard.
Excellent radar finds and centers second-level targets.
Stays connected to the block through positioning and foot chop.
Keeps head on a swivel and can mirror rushers to either edge.
Ready, independent hands search and find pass-pro targets.
Outstanding reactive quickness to recover and secure in both phases.
weaknesses: Lacks desired length and mass for guard spot.
Plays with excessive pre-strike movement in run game.
Positional blocker with little to no drive strength in his lowers.
Baited out of position by Baylor with delayed blitz games.
Catches instead of punching and lacks redirect power on his edges.
Might not be able to withstand NFL bull rushers without help.

strengths: NFL size with shredded frame and real top-end speed.
Quality click-and-close from the top of his drop.
Faces up and searches for the ball in man coverage downfield.
Explosiveness to hammer pass-catchers and jar the ball free.
Has shown a willingness to play through pain.
weaknesses: Inconsistency was a problem throughout college career.
Lacked control and balance in press cut-offs.
Hitch shows up in lateral transitions from press.
Inconsistent to identify and trigger from side shuffle.
Too many finesse attempts as a run-support tackler.

strengths: Connected at an 84% career clip on kicks beyond 50 yards.
Unflappable in 2024, hitting the ball with consistency throughout.
Hit 21 of 22 field goals in 2024, including all seven from 50+ yards.
Made all seven kicks against rivals Michigan State and Ohio State as a Wolverine.
weaknesses: Sub-80% make rate on kicks inside of 40 yards since 2023.
Inconsistent lift off his foot opens him to blocked kicks.
Appeared to lose confidence and aim intermediate kicks from the hashes.

strengths: Long arms with big hands.
Explosive power in both his upper and lower body.
Impressive reaction quickness to prevent being reached by zone blockers.
Pairs a slide step and arm-over move to create gap penetration.
Sturdy base makes him hard to clear from the A-gaps.
Uses anchor and spin counters to ward off wash-down blocks.
Long arms help him get hands on runners to slow their momentum through the line.
weaknesses: Needs to prove he can slide with lateral blocks with squared pads.
Inconsistent hand usage in taking on double-teams.
Lacks consistent shed quickness to get rid of the block.
Has been a non-factor as a pass rusher.
Average body control.

strengths: Uses play strength to create space at the top of his route.
Good feel for when to settle into space against zone coverage.
Separates late and walls off defenders.
Catches with strong hands and good extension when contested.
Challenging to bring down after the catch.
Size and toughness to help as a run blocker.
weaknesses: Will struggle to cleanly elude press.
Runs routes with little speed variance.
Below-average burst from route stem.
Cornerbacks stay in his pocket on go routes.
Below-average separation quickness underneath.
Showed subpar speed at his pro day.

strengths: Frame is compact, girthy and broad.
Plus play strength with hand leverage at the point of attack.
Good short-area foot quickness and athleticism.
Booming club strikes help knock blockers off-balance.
Stays on his feet and searches for the ball through contact.
Brute force to constrict the pocket with his bull rush.
weaknesses: Doesn’t operate with disruptive first-step quickness.
Shorter arms with average block control as two-gapper.
Lacks hip sink to defend and defeat angle blocks.
Would like to see better knock-back pop into first contact.
Fails to gain ground and threaten edges as a pass rusher.

strengths: Elite frame looks like it was assembled in a quarterback factory.
Has experience in a vertically-based passing attack.
Has velocity to sit down and drive the ball into windows.
Throws come out hot, seemingly with very little effort.
Can make every pro throw and push it deep if needed.
Feels edge pressure and will climb/slide inside the pocket.
Potential shows in 2024 games against Oregon and Washington.
Capable of stacking more yardage on his scrambles once he voids.
weaknesses: Lack of rhythm and vision cause him to overlook open targets.
Inconsistent processing coverage.
Struggles to come off pre-snap ideas and alter his plan.
Poise too frequently disappears in the face of pressure.
Willing to throw the ball up instead of throwing it away.
Has trouble keeping receivers in stride with his placement.
Floats deep safeties into high-safety coverages.
Throws with shaky feet and forms weak platforms unnecessarily.

strengths: Highly athletic with fluid hips and basketball player-like foot quickness.
Rarely beaten to the angle on back-side cutoff blocks.
Can shine as a lead blocker on wide runs or in space on screen passes.
Hasty but accurate on work-up blocks from first- to second-level targets.
Smooth lateral pass slides with punch-ready hands.
Delivers pass punch with suddenness, timing and accuracy.
Impressive recovery talent to reconnect with a loose pass rusher.
weaknesses: Below-average play strength shows up in both phases.
Struggles to stay engaged with edge defenders at the point.
Oversteps lateral landmarks, allowing opponents to beat him inside.
Gives ground quickly against speed-to-power rush.
Slow to recognize incoming T/E twists.
Rushers can collapse his outside edge.

strengths: Explosive entry into the kick with a twitchy leg whip.
Generates instant lift with his strikes.
Set Iowa career record with 12 field goals of 50+ yards, including a 58-yarder in 2025.
Athletic and tough to pursue and tackle when returns leak free.
weaknesses: Missed four kicks over a four-game span in 2025.
Has had five kicks blocked during career.
Ball flight can be a little inconsistent.

strengths: Run/catch versatility from backfield or slot.
Uses lead blocks effectively and smoothly changes directions.
Drops hips and cuts sharply downhill from jet sweeps.
Outstanding hand strength to secure and finish the catch.
Breaks off routes with adequate sharpness.
Durable run finisher behind his pads.
weaknesses: Below-average burst to spring past first-level defenders.
Lacks speed to outflank run pursuit or push coverage onto its heels.
Not enough juice out of breaks to shake man coverage.
Short arms limit opportunities to nab off-target throws.
Needs to improve at carving out catch space through force.

strengths: Carries above-average strength and explosiveness.
Builds enough speed to attack seams and run slot fades.
Shows good zone awareness and finds open pockets.
Unbothered by company and wins most contested throws.
Conceals catch intentions and separates late downfield.
Hips-and-hands blocker with consistent placement.
Runs feet through contact to create block sustain.
Displays desired grit and strain as an in-line blocker.
weaknesses: Runs with tight hips and shorter strides.
Doesn’t feature the wiggle to consistently add yards after catch.
Average hand quickness to snare fastballs off-frame.
Average adjusting to moving linebackers as a run blocker.
Tore the ACL in his right knee twice (December 2021 and October 2022).

strengths: Adjusts to route redirection and can beat linebackers down the seam.
Displayed good in-and-out quickness from his breaks versus Texas.
Hands-catcher who excels in bringing in throws from outside his frame.
Tracks downfield throws, adjusting speed and positioning accordingly.
Seven-foot high-jumper who can leap, extend and catch at the highest point.
Dangerous acceleration and elusiveness to add yards after the catch.
Ability to stalk and mirror as a positional blocker in the slot.
weaknesses: Coasts into route breaks and drifts instead of making crisp cuts.
Bad habit of letting off the gas and shirking his true play speed.
Can be accosted at the top of the route by handsy coverage.
Inconsistent to claim his catch space on contested throws.
Commitment and aggression as a run blocker is not where it needs to be.
Has no special-teams experience.

strengths: Torched Oregon twice last season, posting 15 catches for 196 yards and three TDs against the Ducks.
Rugged and urgent when battling against physical coverage.
Disciplined route-running disguises his route intentions.
Speed changes and crisp cuts create space at route breaks.
Uses big frame and catch-space strength to dominate 50/50s.
Dominated back-shoulder balls and slot fades.
Tough to bring him down with the ball in his hands.
Deadens legs and dots feet inbounds at the boundary.
weaknesses: Tight strider with below-average release quickness.
Will find blockades against stronger NFL press.
Noticeable loss of speed through stems and turns.
Downfield routes are easily phased by good man coverage.
Lacks second gear to run under a deep throw.
Labors opening hips and makes reactive, off-frame catches.

strengths: Unique ability to return kicks and cover punts.
Above-average talent and pro potential as a gunner.
No matter the route, the drive phase looks the same.
Long strides create separation on slot fades and deep overs.
Good athlete who can make fluid catch adjustments.
Gives effort with his one-on-one blocks in space.
weaknesses: Only one season of significant catch production.
Slender frame with smaller hands.
Needs to improve hand-fighting to fight off press.
Excessive gather steps on intermediate comebacks.
Below-average catch consistency and technique.

strengths: Tall, long-limbed and rangy as a down safety.
Adequate eye balance and anticipation from off coverages.
Triggers to the action with long, ground-gaining strides.
Frequently delivers strikes on pass-catchers.
Size and toughness to bolster run defense from the box.
Slips blocks and slithers through traffic deep into run lanes.
Uses his expansive tackle radius to run and capture in space.
“Peanut punched” his way to nine career forced fumbles.
weaknesses: Excessive bouncing and movement during run diagnosis.
Needs to settle feet sooner to prevent open-field misses.
Downhill dives open him to loss of containment against the run.
Won’t be a versatile coverage piece on the back end.
Inefficient footwork when covering in space.
Will struggle staying connected to vertical speed.

strengths: Broad build with excellent lean muscle mass, long arms and huge hands.
Does a nice job of adjusting and fitting blocks on short pulls.
Plays with precise hand placement and a strong base in run game.
Glass-eating field demeanor will be appreciated by coaches.
Powerful in upper body to corral and sustain against movers.
Plays the game with consistent forward push and drive power.
Able to short-set and blockade the pocket versus power.
Protects with active hands to stay in the face of the rush.
weaknesses: Lacks ideal initial quickness into the neutral zone.
Needs better attention to his secure block on combos.
Hand quickness is average for first strikes in run game.
Below-average change of direction and adjustments in space.
Occasionally overstrides in his protection mirror.
Pushes instead of punching, creating imbalance versus counters.

strengths: His volume, motor and stamina give defenses no snaps off.
Able to make downhill cuts at top speed.
Hot tempo prevents two-gappers from shedding blocks on time to tackle.
Stutter-stepping and dancing is not in his vocabulary.
Adamant north-south running style punishes defenders who miss run fits.
Big part of the passing game with soft hands and easy adjustments.
weaknesses: Segmented runner lacking rhythm and flow.
Average top-end speed and contact balance.
Tunnel-visioned at times and lacks creativity to reset his track.
Average feel for managing space when getting through the lane.
Lacks size and power to run through contact with force.
Takes a beat to accelerate after lateral cuts.

strengths: Big NFL frame with thick chest and long arms.
Plays with controlled footwork and balance into first contact.
Booming upper-body power when he unloads into opponents.
Solid initial slide quickness to hit diagonal set-points.
Grip and core strength help him latch and control the rush.
weaknesses: Tight hips limit leverage as drive blocker and anchor in pass pro.
Takes an excessive number of settle steps when fitting into run blocks.
Below-average change of direction to hit moving targets in space.
Gathers rushers with length instead of punching and disrupting the rush.
Inside moves and counters come too quickly for him to match.

strengths: Has length and timing between hands/feet for adequate press.
Sees route development and adjusts zone coverage accordingly.
Splits zone positioning effectively against high/low concepts.
Capable of crowding and smothering early stages of the route.
Keeps inside arm ready to shade passing lane from trail technique.
Tracks down vertical routes and shallow crossers with make-up speed.
Typically maintains contain positioning in run support.
weaknesses: Disappointing number of takeaway chances despite his size/length.
Upright and lacking proper balance in break-point transitions.
Feet linger when getting out of the top of his drop from a pedal.
Needs to become more aggressive with coverage positioning.
Finesse tackler in run support.

strengths: Carries a wide wingspan with 35-inch arms.
Productive season as a tackler, with long levers to circumvent blocks.
When he’s near the route, the squeeze is on.
Threatening closing burst to the quarterback as a blitzer.
Long frame allows him to make tackles from distance.
Actively targets punch-outs, forcing five fumbles in 2025.
weaknesses: Must add functional mass to play in the league.
Gets lifted up and out of the gap by linemen.
Lacks upper-body power and hand usage to get off blocks.
Below-average change of direction in man coverage.
High center of gravity makes open-field tackling tougher.

strengths: Tall receiver with outstanding length
Speed to sound the alarm for defenses.
Uses a late leverage step to clear his break-point.
Long strides gain sudden separation out of stems.
Stacks corners, creating larger landing strips for deep throws.
Activates hands late to keep face-guarders guessing.
Explodes for yards after catch with get-away juice.
weaknesses: Lacks ideal footwork in short spaces.
Rounded routes make it tougher to separate.
Tilts pads prematurely, giving away break-points.
Body-catcher with below-average hand-eye coordination.
Begins coasting when he turns to find the football.
Doesn’t run to his size on quick catch-and-run throws.

strengths: Plays with high effort on every snap.
Hands are quiet and efficient in unlocking rush angles.
Possesses lower-body drive to push through edges and into pocket.
Fights hard to stay tight to the rush arc when redirected at the top.
Instant read-and-react to match zone steps by offensive linemen.
Good skill level and feel for working around base blocks.
Has the mentality and traits to become a core special-teamer.
weaknesses: Doesn’t appear to have much of a rush plan.
Needs to set up winning rush moves with better purpose.
One-speed pass rush lacks instinctive counters when stalled.
Driven off the mark and out of the picture by angle blocks from tight end.
Lacks pursuit speed to close from the back side or squeeze play-side corner.

strengths: Explosive 2024 season, posting 1,172 receiving yards and 12 touchdowns.
Eats up the cushion quickly when he needs to.
Tempos into acceleration at the stem on deep routes.
Disguises route breaks with a consistently quiet upper body.
Maintains speed while locating and tracking the throw.
Displayed ability to return punts in 2025.
weaknesses: Drops cost him touchdowns in both 2024 and 2025.
Deceleration and change-of-direction fluidity are average.
Inconsistent balance and footwork in and out of intermediate breaks.
Doesn’t get much after the catch in tighter spaces.
Missed 2022 season with a shoulder injury.

strengths: Has the hand size of a defensive lineman and a 6-foot-7 wingspan.
Can side-step lead blocks or take them on with good aggression.
Length allows him a margin for error as a tackler.
Experienced in special-teams coverage.
Confirmed his impressive range/speed at pro day.
weaknesses: Has a long frame but noticeably thin in his lower half.
Leggy lateral movement hinders his fluidity.
Gets outside shoulder covered and loses contain leverage.
Inconsistent locating the football in both phases.
Below-average awareness and route squeeze from zone.

strengths: Above-average height, length and hand size for an edge rusher.
Plays with ideal aggression.
Drives hands through tackles and tight ends on first contact.
Good job of separating, locating the ball and shedding the block.
Slippery through the rush edge once he finds an opening.
Blocked three field goals in the last two seasons.
weaknesses: Plays upright and with inconsistent base width.
Lean through his lowers and lacks quality anchor.
Will struggle holding the point against most double-teams.
Below-average first-step quickness and rush sequencing.
Hasn’t developed go-to moves or workable rush counters.
Linear closer who struggles to make path alterations versus QB movement.

strengths: Good feel for angles to help leverage his run gap.
Efficient with movement and timing working his combos.
Drives feet through contact to smother opponent’s space.
Plays with good strength and strain at the point of attack.
Strikes bull rushers with quick resets for leverage.
Agile, quick feet to recover across his gap in protection.
weaknesses: Stubby build lacks ideal size and length.
Needs to play with more consistent hand placement.
Defenders play around his lack of length at times.
Sees his punch wiped quickly away by long defenders.
Struggles getting edge leakage redirected in protection.
Seventh-year senior who will be an older rookie.

strengths: NFL body type with broad frame and good thickness in arms/legs.
Creates leverage with upward hand strikes into base blocks.
Digs hands in and runs feet through the finish line as a down blocker.
Choppy, smooth pass slides take him to proper set-points.
Uses quick, independent hands to feint and connect.
Delivers a tight, crisp punch with inside hands.
weaknesses: Loses most battles for pad level at the point of attack.
Below-average footwork to improve and/or lock in block positioning.
Tightness in lower half limits lateral quickness for zone blocks.
A stout bull rush can bounce him back into the pocket.
Slow to slide and mirror inside rush moves.
Rarely clamps up rusher and allows him to ignite a secondary rush.

strengths: Capable of playing all three interior spots.
Digs hands into place and churns through contact on down blocks.
Good work rate and effort through the whistle.
Slides tight to teammates to squeeze gaps and fortify edges.
Adequate recognition and response time against twists.
weaknesses: Slow hands allow opponents to quickly punch and separate.
Has trouble landing flush on targets in space.
Inconsistent re-leveraging his hands when needed.
Hunches forward in pass pro, opening himself to counters.
Anchor is a little shallow when challenged by bull rushers.

strengths: Ultra durable and the son of a coach.
Goes full blocking sled on down blocks with powerful leg drive.
Strong with willingness to brawl in the sustain phase.
Accelerates into crunching blows on pulling blocks.
Light on his feet with easy slides and redirects in pass pro.
Opens ankles and can bounce into his anchor.
weaknesses: Leans into his pass punch to compensate for short arms.
Rides tackle twists too long and too far into gaps.
Base becomes excessively wide in pass sets, opening him to counters.
Needs more consistent hand placement and quicker resets.
Below-average body control in the midst of hand-to-hand combat.
Narrow with his block control, allowing opponents to leak off his edge.

strengths: Team captain with a wealth of experience.
Very rugged demeanor with a taste for physicality.
Power to treat tight ends who line up against him shabbily.
Strong core and base help him withstand power.
Always looking to get to the ball and refuses to ride the block.
Uses bend-and-rip move to hug the rush arc against redirects.
Violent chop knocks the tackle’s outside punch hand away.
Works back underneath at pocket depth to vacuum pocket-climbers.
weaknesses: Lacks ideal twitch getting in and out of his block engagements.
Needs to play with better discipline for contain positioning.
Average short-area quickness for sudden tackle tries.
Rush path tends to be monotonous and predictable for tackles.
Not much speed variance or angle changes in his rush.
Needs to set up inside counters on the next level.

strengths: Stays square for a good while in his pass sets.
Finds work as a clean-up man when he’s uncovered.
Despite his height, comes off the ball with good pad level.
Rips upward hand strikes into defender’s frame at contact.
Able to unlock lower half and roll hips into the block.
Plays until he hears the whistle and looks to finish with authority.
weaknesses: Below-average muscle mass and definition throughout his upper body.
Can be knocked back by an average bull rush inside.
Slow reaction time dooms him when trying to recover against rushers crossing his face.
Inconsistent footwork to match slanting fronts and fit up his block.
Struggles badly to stay centered and sustain as both a run and pass blocker.
Poor change of direction in space when asked to make climbing/pulling blocks.

strengths: Flashes from 2024 offer insight into his potential.
Impressive arm talent opens up opportunities.
Able to zip spirals into very tight windows.
Plays through pain and is tough in the face of imminent contact.
Plays with an admirable swagger and fearlessness.
Operational under pressure, with ability to make a play.
Bounces and climbs to avoid the rush and get to a launch point.
Takes very few unnecessary sacks.
weaknesses: Smaller than average with durability concerns.
Will force a circus throw when it’s not necessary.
Disguised coverages will bog down his process.
Has trouble getting away from predetermined decisions.
Puts the ball at risk far too often with low-reward throws.
Shakes off changeups and throws the gas too often.

strengths: Dropped weight, adding quickness and production to his game.
Built like a dump truck with a broad chest and girthy lower half.
Stay square and firm through lateral block engagements.
Twitchy upper body allows for violent block sheds.
Heavy base grows deep roots when taking on double teams.
Short but peeks past the block to quickly locate the runner.
Absorbs angle blocks and battles back into position.
weaknesses: Frequently beaten to lateral landmarks and sealed by center.
Below-average hand quickness and placement into blockers.
Ends up on the ground too often.
Lacks range to make plays beyond a gap away.
Flailing arms and legs generate no momentum when rushing.

strengths: Length and instincts create on-ball opportunities.
Route recognition to mirror routes underneath.
Keeps eyes to the quarterback to adjust his coverage.
Makes positive plays when overlapping his coverage.
Long strides erase distance quickly when closing.
Snakes hands around receivers and into catch-points without drawing penalty flags.
Has size and length to become a better wrap-up tackler.
weaknesses: Technique and footwork get loose on the back end.
Sharp turns from deep safety spot are slow and labored.
Struggles sustaining his long speed in pursuit.
Leggy with average body control and stop-start quickness.
Unreliable in run support and as a tackler in general.
Broken tackles allow big add-on yardage.

strengths: Outstanding frame with proportional build and good lean mass.
Throws heavy hand strikes into first contact in the run game.
Clears out down blocks with aggressive finishes.
Knocks linebackers around on second level
Held up admirably against Penn State rush talent.
Protection posture features a tucked chin and flat back.
Able to sink eventual anchor after being bounced early.
weaknesses: Segmented mover getting from block to block on combos.
Inconsistent pad level and centering getting into blocks.
Needs better hand placement to prevent slipping off of blocks.
Below-average punch timing gets him stabbed and rocked back into pocket.
Heavy legs and tight hips hinder ability to mirror and recover when needed.
Can be caught off-guard and out of position against twists.

strengths: Prototype physique paired with a fearless playing style.
Uses long leap past tackle’s set point with hopping cross-chop.
Gains tremendous ground on his Euro-step counter inside.
Deep bender capable of cornering tightly at the turn.
Willing to do the dirty work to spill or contain the action.
Springy and agile in all directions to pursue and tackle.
Impressive athlete in space with the ability to drop and cover.
weaknesses: Edge-setting lacks proper fundamentals.
Leads into contact helmet-first with hands lagging behind.
Blockers can get into his frame and create movement.
Hasn’t put together his bull-rush technique just yet.
Possesses a bucket of moves but lacks a cohesive rush plan.
Hot in pursuit, causing him to lose leverage versus cutback runs.

strengths: Good size, big hands and inside/outside versatility.
Tempo alterations help to bypass and separate from coverage.
Able to drop hips and quickly snap off curls/comebacks.
Smooth strider with steady turnover working through zone.
Gets through route transitions smoothly and at top speed.
Above-average focus and footwork near the sideline.
Weaponized as a utility option out of the backfield.
Plus field vision and cut quickness when the ball is in his hands.
weaknesses: Needs better physicality and hand-fighting to defeat press.
Accelerates quickly but isn't a vertical burner.
Positioning would benefit from quicker adjustments.
Needs to become more consistently competitive for catch space.
Most drops come when working into the hashes.
Average feel for coverage positioning on zone beaters.

strengths: Able to get skinny and quickly penetrate into gaps.
Tracks ball-carriers and slides toward their lane choice.
Slips off block engagement with active hands and loose upper body.
Above-average reactive quickness as a tackler.
Foot agility and body control are made for twists and rush games.
Searches relentlessly for a crevice to access the pocket.
Edge-to-edge rush attack with good closing speed to get home.
weaknesses: Plays too straight-legged and lacks leverage at the point.
Lands hands quickly but struggles to control and shed blocks.
Lacks mass/anchor to consistently leverage his gap.
Inconsistent in maintaining run fits on lateral engagements.
Rush advantages can be muted by athletic guards.
Needs hand usage to catch up with footwork in pass rush.

strengths: Posted 35.5 tackles for loss, 17 sacks and eight forced fumbles in his four seasons.
Reads run and scrapes to the line to compress the front.
Lands a crisp two-hand punch to stay clean against blocks.
Adequate burst in short spaces to close on lane development.
Sifts through route development to locate zone landmarks.
Triggers a well-timed blitz to harass the pocket.
Accelerates through contact to finish tackles near the line.
Was a special-teams demon his freshman season.
weaknesses: Average burst to chase and squeeze runs to the boundary.
Needs to be quicker in defeating skill-position blockers.
Linear pursuit to the football leads to missed tackles.
Inconsistent breaking down in time as an open-field tackler.
Too much confusion on his coverage tape.
Average elusiveness to bypass blitz pickup.

strengths: Played deep into the College Football Playoff at both Penn State and Ole Miss.
Made nine grabs for 156 yards and a score in playoff win over Georgia.
Head fakes create some movement from opposing corners.
Good urgency when working zone-beaters from the slot.
Sharpens his catch focus when working into traffic.
Plenty of toughness to finish catches through contact.
Climbs the ladder to find high-point positioning.
weaknesses: Below-average foot quickness to consistently slip press.
Lack of bend causes drift and loss of momentum at turns.
Below-average footwork and burst at break points.
Inconsistent making late course adjustments on deep throws.
Not enough run-after-catch talent.

strengths: Resilient with good mental toughness for the position.
Improved his efficiency as a game manager since 2024.
Continues scanning for completions when play extends.
Adequate touch to layer throws when needed.
Capable runner who can pick up tough yards on called runs.
Finished career with 37 rushing touchdowns.
weaknesses: High-cut with long legs and lean frame.
Will need to play with better ball security.
Accuracy and ball placement fall below the mark.
Footwork will become wild when pressured in the pocket.
Has issues overstriding and lacks drive velocity.

strengths: Able to mirror the release and shadow pattern breaks from trail.
Stiff jabs at full extension slow receivers and disrupt route timing.
Good eye balance between quarterback and route concepts in zone.
Has the long speed to carry vertical routes downfield.
Drives on seam throws and overlaps coverage from zone.
Takes playmaking angles to either force turnovers or disrupt the catch point.
Led the nation with 18 passes defensed, including five interceptions.
weaknesses: Inconsistent balance and long legs create slower transitions in space.
Phases on the top of verticals, creating easier comeback throws.
Gets leveraged into prematurely declaring his hips.
Grabs, tugs and mauls downfield instead of trusting technique.
Needs to turn and locate the football from tight man.
Shies away from proper tackling form against big running backs.

strengths: Plays with good eye balance between quarterback and routes.
Finished career with 25 passes defensed and 11 interceptions.
Plays downhill into run fits with a steady pace.
Stays wide and tackle-ready in the hole.
weaknesses: Allows runs to cross his face and escape wide.
Inconsistent tackle finisher when trying to bring down receivers after the catch.
Below-average range and field speed to maintain man coverage.
Underwent left shoulder surgery in December.

strengths: Extensive game experience as a four-year starter.
Operates with heavy hands in run and pass blocking.
Displays ability to adjust and connect to movement in space.
Independent hands help create contact with rush.
Rifles a very stiff left jab into rusher with ideal timing.
Uses length to redirect rusher outside intended rush path.
weaknesses: Tight-hipped with high pads and narrow base.
Gets beat inside due to oversetting.
Footwork and recoveries are messy at times.
Feet and hands lack harmony and lead to lunging.
Sloppy hand placement prevents ideal block control.
Lack of fluidity diminishes timing for combo blocks.

strengths: Logged 418 carries for 2,208 yards and 29 TDs since start of 2024 season.
Presses the line deep to coax linebackers into traffic.
Sustains top speed to run away and hide in the open field.
Delivers right shoulder like a battering ram to thwart tacklers.
Sniffs out creases to punch through near the end zone.
Can line up in the slot or out wide to mismatch linebackers.
Squares and anchors versus blitzers and mirrors post-contact.
weaknesses: Misses opportunities to burst wide and outflank contain.
Too many hops and wasted steps early in the run.
Average lateral jump cuts to avoid penetration.
Can be a herky-jerky runner getting from cut to cut.
Focus drops on swing passes were an issue in 2025.
Slow to accelerate up the field after the catch.

strengths: Tall, fast and long with ability to play “X” or “Z” spots.
Former sprinter with sustained deep speed to separate vertically.
Has the footwork to run a varied route tree on all three levels.
Clever in outfoxing the coverage and defeating his leverage.
Size and toughness to protect catches through punishing hits.
Ball skills to outclass corners when contested outside the numbers.
Good post-catch elusiveness to tack on yards after catch underneath.
weaknesses: Never came close to replicating his catch production from 2022.
Below-average salesmanship to tilt defenders at break points.
Average quickness when gathering and opening on comebacks.
Plays with some route drift, making it easier on the coverage.
Run blocking could use just a little more strain and grit.

strengths: Carries good size with massive wingspan and big hands.
Good timing and consistency on his pin-and-climb combos.
Power to torque and turn defender out of run gaps.
Drives feet and looks to finish blocks when he has the advantage.
Uses vertical sets with awareness to the B-gap against T/E twists.
Patience and independent hands allow him to gather spin counters.
Good athleticism to mirror inside moves and find targets in space on screens.
weaknesses: Leads with his helmet and upper body far too often.
Body control and contact balance are below average.
Poor hand placement limits block sustain/consistency in both phases.
Opens outside hip way too early against speed rushers.
Allows rushers into his chest without popping and gathering.
Lethargic hands allow rushers to counter and eliminate the punch.

strengths: Runs downfield routes with gliding strides.
Shows plus catch focus and creates late separation when contested.
Gets easy wins when pinning corners on fades.
Makes impressive late adjustments for quick grabs.
Long arms and big hands created viral catches.
Light on his feet to slip first the tackler after the catch.
weaknesses: Departure will be delayed by a jamming press.
Routes are typically run at a single speed.
Slow to break off and open quickly on stops and comebacks.
Coverage tends to blanket him through lateral break-points.
Really struggles to play through route contact.

strengths: Possesses a good blend of size, speed and strength.
Good peripheral vision and awareness of incoming angle blocks.
Uses proactive hands to strike and separate on the second level.
Dips low and wraps around the legs as an interior tackler.
Has the traits to compete for a special-teams role.
weaknesses: Average instincts and trigger quickness.
Makes mistakes with run fits and gets caught in the trash.
Average bend diminishes ability to leverage his gap through contact.
Would benefit from better pacing and patience with lateral scrapes.
Lacks fluidity in coverage situations.

strengths: Started every game over his three seasons.
Led Cal in receiving in 2024 as Fernando Mendoza’s top target.
Plays through contact at the release and at the top of the route.
Builds speed to stress man coverage on intermediate routes.
Bodies up tight man coverage and catches with hand extension.
Sudden hands to stab and secure hot or high throws.
Adjusts and centers defensive ends on split-zone blocks.
Stays after blocks in space once he connects.
weaknesses: Needs more functional mass and play strength.
Run-blocking angles and landmarks are inconsistent.
Below-average quickness in and out of breaks.
Few drops, but too many double-catches in 2024.
Lacks make-you-miss elusiveness and reacceleration after the catch.

strengths: Delivers firm punch on climbing blocks.
Adequate speed for most sideline-to-sideline pursuits.
Possesses quality play strength and some nasty in his demeanor.
Quick to close and pounce on pass-catcher from man/zone.
Displays good feel as a blitzer and is able to trim the corner off the edge.
Has plug-and-play traits as a four-phase special-teamer.
weaknesses: Average eye quickness and reaction time to his keys.
Will turn his pads and overrun his fits against wide runs.
Needs to leverage his gaps through better positioning.
Inconsistent technique and execution to get rid of a block.
Has some trouble finishing tackles with consistency in space.
Was a non-factor against the Texas Tech running game.

strengths: Densely muscled with thick limbs and a burly neck.
Sturdy base, upper-body strength and contact balance to battle at the point of attack.
Plays through and around blocking tight ends with explosive hands.
Gets off blocks and hustles to the ball to tackle.
Activates counter spin underneath when he reaches pocket depth.
Plenty of experience on special teams.
weaknesses: Hasn’t shown the knack or ability for getting to the quarterback.
Lacks go-to moves or hand counters to create opportunities.
Easy to find, punch and widen to take him out of the rush.
Inconsistent tracking play development in the backfield.
Below-average length to punch and separate from NFL tackles.

strengths: Hang-time improved with each season.
Delivers directional punts with relative consistency.
Makes the ball cut or deaden on his nose-drop punts.
Punts with adequate power when backed up.
weaknesses: Might need to quicken his operation time as a pro.
Allows too many returns as an open-field punter.
Greedy on pooch punts, pushing them up against the goal line.

strengths: Compact frame with plus agility and overall athleticism.
Moves with good lateral quickness off the snap.
Capable of becoming a pest in the gaps if coached for it.
Adequate power in hips and hands to attack/neutralize base blocks.
Explosive closing speed in the pocket and down the line.
Rush hands are heavy enough to batter and dislodge the punch.
weaknesses: Needs to become more willing to take on the heavy lifting against the run.
Ducks helmet and leans into first contact at the point of attack.
Didn’t show enough physicality against Kansas State’s O-line.
Should be creating more consistent chaos with his tools.
Hasn’t developed enough go-to moves or rush counters.
Too many centered rush attempts stifle his production.

strengths: Good size with impressive tackle production in 2025.
Posted NFL-caliber testing numbers at his pro day.
Good patience to wait and mirror runner’s cuts against zone blocks.
Plays firm behind his pads and with good physicality.
Gets low, squares up and wraps the legs as a tackler.
weaknesses: Only one season with significant production.
Lacks proportional wingspan relative to his height.
Needs to become more proactive attacking blocking schemes.
Below average take-on technique at the point of attack.
Below average lateral agility and foot quickness in space.

strengths: Combines high tackle totals with plus on-ball production.
Posted 13 career forced fumbles, including FBS-leading seven in 2025.
Keys quickly and races to the junction point.
Rarely caught flat-footed in his lateral pursuit.
Constant movement makes him hard to hit for linemen.
Won’t drift deep into traffic without a plan of action.
Punches and plays off the block with excellent technique.
Has the instincts, eyes and ball skills of a greedy safety in coverage.
Field command and performance inspire those around him.
weaknesses: Can be knocked around inside the box by size.
Will occasionally false-step in the diagnose stage.
Will take a big bite on misdirection and play-action.
Coverage busts were costly in 2025.
Comes in hot to the catch, leading to overruns.
Lacks desired length for consistent wrap-ups.

strengths: Racked up 125 tackles – including 13 for loss – in 2025.
Has room on his frame for more muscle mass.
Rare length at linebacker with a 7-foot wingspan.
Has the range and athleticism to successfully track wide runs.
Effectively uses his length in wrapping and finishing in the open field.
Improved his feel for zone coverage as last season progressed.
weaknesses: Long-limbed but still too lean in his lower half.
Struggles to consistently read keys and diagnose play design.
Lacks base strength to maintain balance through contact.
Hasn’t learned to leverage his length against climbing blocks.
Once blocked, tends to stay blocked.

strengths: Smooth transitions playing from a pedal or shuffle.
Impressive closing acceleration to receivers and throws.
Light on his feet anticipating and shading break points.
Very decisive and committed in his coverage decisions.
High-end competitor who brings real juice to the field.
Rarely out of phase, ending catches with immediacy.
Hustles into contain positioning when action spills wide.
weaknesses: Possesses a slender frame with short arms.
Displays small delays flipping hips and sprinting.
Gets knocked around at the top of the route by size.
Lack of length could limit interception opportunities.
Will have trouble ungluing from big perimeter blockers.
Drag-down tackler, lacking desired stopping power.

strengths: Elite body composition with lean mass and a huge wingspan.
Possesses rare strength.
Even with subpar technique, he can move defensive players.
Gets easy wash downs on angle blocks when catching them flush.
Upper-body power can stop the rush cold when hands lock in.
Rush attempts down the middle will go nowhere.
weaknesses: Footwork fitting up his blocks is uneven and unsound.
Below-average body control and bend at the point and in space.
Ends up on the grass way more than he should.
Gaming fronts create confusion and busts in protection.
Takes quick losses when rushers counter his lunging.
Dealt with injuries in each of the last two years.

strengths: Hits NFL marks for measurables at his position.
Wins the lateral race to his zone landmarks.
Climbs quickly with athletic adjustments in space.
Uses core power and grip strength to secure his blocks.
Alters punch timing and approach, using independent hands.
Adequate anchor drop to slow bull rushers when tested.
weaknesses: Weight gets too far forward as a run blocker.
Pressed and pulled aside by two-gappers.
Gets beaten into the frame more than he wins.
Ducks helmet into contact with pass rushers.
Has troubling redirecting long rushers off his edge.

strengths: Impresses off the bus with prototypical size, length and musculature.
Pairs disruptive traits and playing style with a salty demeanor.
Capable of lining up as an inside linebacker or stand-up edge.
Delivers knock-back pop to blockers and can quickly shed.
Plays through or around blocks without losing positioning.
His first three steps are sudden and threatening as a rusher.
Dips, rips and flattens the edge against slower tackles.
Elite bend and reactive agility allow him to tackle from unlikely angles.
Smooth and rangy with his spot drops in coverage.
Remarkable stop-start quickness when changing direction.
weaknesses: Takes chances inside that lead to loss of contain for the defense.
Frenetic playing style can lead to missteps and missed tackles.
Buzzes over top of the pocket instead of finding the drop depth.
Rush features reactive movement but lacks instinctive hand counters.
Aggression can work against him with 15-yard penalties.

strengths: Explosive athlete with impressive size.
Attacks blocks and constricts run flow with heavy hands.
Scrapes with tempo alterations to leverage the run.
Effective use of hands to play off blockers and make sudden tackles.
Comes to balance and has good stopping power as a tackler.
Not easily manipulated out of his zone by the quarterback’s eyes.
Can immediately step in as a kick-cover talent.
weaknesses: Average quickness to diagnose and read the play design.
Will work under blocks and lose the angle on occasion.
Needs a quicker downhill trigger to blow up the blocking scheme.
Long legs with average short-area agility in coverage.
Could struggle covering running backs if asked.
Linear pass rusher with below-average elusiveness.

strengths: Accelerates and runs feet through contact on outside zone blocks.
Has ability to reach and seal play-side 1-techniques.
Quick eyes allow him to adjust assignments suddenly when needed.
Typically strains through the block until he gets to the finish line.
Light on his feet to slide and protect against edge-to-edge rushers.
Lands firm hands into pass rusher to set the tone for the rep.
weaknesses: Narrow shoulders with below-average mass for the position.
Rarely gets much displacement when asked to drive block.
Has to guard against lunging at linebackers on second level.
Occasionally a step slow responding to incoming twisters.
Might not have the anchor to handle bull rushers heads-up.

strengths: Disguises intentions, dropping down late as an add-on defender.
Times up snap count to rocket off the edge and into the backfield.
Ready and willing to explode into lead blocks.
Triggers from depth to meet the action near the line.
Career production includes 16 pass breakups and nine interceptions.
Above-average range and reaction as short-zone defender.
Sees block and route development with impressive peripheral vision.
weaknesses: Allows receivers to get behind him more than he should.
Burst to close and make-up speed in coverage are below average.
Loses pursuit leverage when he’s too hurried running the alley.
Tight-hipped and lacking desired lateral burst and agility.
Ignites his tackle try before closing enough distance.

strengths: Violent striker.
Gets his head around to locate downfield throws.
Plays with good awareness and ball skills.
Toughness to play near the line full-time.
Comes downhill and through tackle targets in run support.
Productive tackler on kick-cover teams.
weaknesses: Stubby build with a lack of ideal wingspan.
Durability and history of shoulder injuries could be a concern.
Lacks acceleration to stay connected to vertical speed.
Below-average length to defend against jump balls.
Hip tightness limits lateral agility as an open-field tackler.

strengths: Stays low with pad level in proper position.
Drives through hips to lift and push base blocks.
Shows off knock-back pop on lead pulls.
Feet stay busy through contact.
Patient with hands and rarely lunges after the rusher.
Gets good extension to maximize length when punching.
Good anchor drop after initial bounce into pocket.
weaknesses: Stays low but can get too far out over his toes.
Hands can drift wide of the frame as a run blocker.
Has trouble finding cut-off angles up to linebackers.
Lacks awareness of developing games in protection.
Needs to keep feet active through his pass punch.
Struggles staying mirrored to and redirecting attacks on his edge.

strengths: First-step quickness to threaten gaps as a penetrator.
Leverage and burst to play through seal blocks laterally.
Drives upward hand strikes into first contact with good pop.
Shows foot quickness to work around base blocks and threaten edges as a rusher.
Was a plus tester across the board at his pro day.
weaknesses: Only one season of production as a full-time starter.
Below-average anchor to sink and fight back against double-teams.
Lacks control and overruns tackles/sacks once he’s in the backfield.
Injury history is part of his evaluation.
Will be a 25-year-old rookie.

strengths: Posted 11 interceptions in the last three seasons.
Aware of route development but plays the quarterback.
Able to slip or defeat blocks near the line of scrimmage.
Runs the alley with timely gathering for control into tackles.
weaknesses: Slow to find the accelerator on his lateral transitions.
Lacks top-end speed to carry routes vertically.
Loses ground and pursuit angles as the run progresses wide.
Struggles making sharp change of direction in downhill run support.

strengths: Shows twitchiness once he triggers.
Good change of direction to flow with routes and runs.
Fights hard to work off of or around blockers.
Very willing and tough in run support.
Targets and wraps the legs to finish off running backs.
Has experience as both a gunner and jammer.
weaknesses: Lone collegiate interception came in 2022.
Below-average instincts and route anticipation.
Struggles to trust his eyes and trigger with timing from zone.
Has trouble finding positioning to play downfield throws.
Mindset for run-support duties but carries a leaner frame.

strengths: Rockets downhill, erasing distance from split-safety perch to support run.
Makes pursuit adjustments on the move to center and strike the runner.
Drives through the running back’s thighs to make quick stops.
Burst to close provides hope for better on-ball production.
Runs through pass-catchers underneath with a full head of steam.
Traits and skill set are made for kick/punt cover teams.
weaknesses: Missed all of 2024 with an injury and made just 12 career starts.
Has trouble navigating traffic caused by bunch formations.
Magnet for play-action fakes and is pulled out of position.
Takes steep angles rushing toward his man-cover duties.
Plays with a lack of technique on the back end.
Rushes into a run lane without allowing the runner to declare.

strengths: Explosive leaper and former Texas state champ in the triple jump.
Quick change of direction to beat man coverage on underneath routes.
Smooth but sudden transitions in his route breaks.
Able to drop hips and open quickly on comeback routes.
Capable of rising tall and pulling in spectacular grabs.
Good feel for boundary, deadening feet to dot the turf.
weaknesses: Unable to do much against talented Stephen F. Austin cornerbacks.
Hasn’t developed an NFL-caliber release versus press.
Needs to work back sooner to underthrown passes.
Doesn’t consistently utilize best play speed in his routes.
Not strong enough as a run blocker.

strengths: Shows ability to throw with adequate anticipation.
Enough arm talent to push the ball vertically when needed.
Throws with decent touch on deep fades.
Plus escapability to break the pocket or extend plays.
Moves the sticks as a scrambler and on designed runs.
Above-average vision and cut quickness as a runner.
weaknesses: Forces throws into no-win windows.
