Poor feel for the range of deep safeties.
Footwork unravels on occasion, even without heavy pressure.
Below-average ball placement on short/intermediate timing throws.
Inconsistent finding and hitting intermediate zone windows.
Disappointing accuracy when extending pass plays.

strengths: Big defensive back with long arms and explosive lowers.
Posted eight interceptions and 21 pass breakups in his three college seasons.
Can stall tight ends and big slot receivers from press.
Rangy zone cover man with good vision and anticipation.
Closes quickly and lands a heavy blow on receivers after the catch.
Swallows receiver with long arms and full wrap when making a tackle.
Has plenty of stopping power as a run defender.
Plus balance and reactive agility as an open-field tackler.
weaknesses: Good play speed for a safety but not for a cornerback.
Average transition burst matching route breaks.
Turns the motor down when he’s away from the play.
Below average playing off of blocks for a player his size.

strengths: Good build with low center of gravity and strong lower half.
Creates lateral space with jump-cut.
Able to plant and cut sharply downhill on wide runs.
Gets through the line with good bend and forward lean.
Uses off hand to ward off closing tacklers in space.
Had success covering kicks and punts in 2023.
weaknesses: Segmented running style slows downhill progress.
Lacks desired vision and feel for lane development.
Below-average burst to outflank defenses and turn the corner.
Indecisive runner with a lack of spatial awareness.
Breaks some tackles but doesn’t create many misses.

strengths: Stocky but well-built and strong in his lower half.
Good stop-start quickness to recalibrate his run.
Shows an ability to make sharp cuts across the grain.
Displays an aptitude for running routes underneath.
Showed rare burst at his pro day.
weaknesses: Production has diminished in consecutive seasons.
Dances and slows when processing block development.
Below-average wiggle to elude second-level tacklers.
Lacks feel for setting up blocks and creating opportunities for himself.
Catches with stiff hands and subpar hand-eye coordination.

strengths: Very precise with initial hand placement as a run blocker.
Keeps feet churning to stay engaged and occupy the defender.
Capable lead puller with the mobility to climb and cut off inside ‘backers.
Plays with steady balance and consistent base width in protection.
Maintains arm extension and feel for A-gap leakage in pass pro.
Inside hands and core strength help him set a firm anchor.
weaknesses: Lacks ideal size and can be mismatched by true 0-techniques.
Lacks pop on contact to set the tone as a run blocker.
Doesn’t generate enough leg drive to displace on single blocks.
Could struggle to stay latched to longer, NFL-caliber two-gappers.
Below-average lateral range to save his edge against athletic rushers.
Needs to stay square and catch-ready when passing off twists.

strengths: His strongest run-stopping performance came versus talented Ohio State team.
Quick read and lateral response to zone steps by O-linemen.
Displays solid pop into first contact.
Lands accurate hands and quickly presses into extension.
Shuffles feet to work his way around turn-out blocks.
Activates his hands early in the rush rep.
weaknesses: Below-average feel for pressure on his edges.
Knocked off balance and out of position by double-teams.
Needs to drop pad level and widen base when battling move blocks.
Tight-hipped with limited lateral strides to work rush edges.
Unable to manufacture enough openings into the pocket.
Suffered an Achilles injury in 2020 and missed all but one game in 2024 with a pectoral injury.

strengths: Impressive catch production for a single season at tight end.
Keeps his pace up throughout the route.
Sits quickly and shows his numbers in short zone.
Excellent hand-eye coordination and catch focus.
Adjustments to off-frame throws come naturally.
Experienced four-phase special-teams option.
weaknesses: Small and lacking length even by H-back standards.
Has trouble separating from man coverage underneath.
Very limited catch radius requires an accurate passer.
Starting at ground floor with run-blocking technique/fundamentals.
Appears to lack willingness for collisions necessary as move blocker.

strengths: Big center with outstanding length and 11-inch hands.
Fits run blocks with good hand placement and base width.
Connects and chops his feet through the whistle.
Good recognition against internal twists and blitzes.
Throws punch with purpose in pass pro.
weaknesses: Below-average snap-to-step quickness in run game.
Movements in space are segmented and clunky.
Struggles adjusting to movement as both run and pass blocker.
Leans and loses against early movement to his edges in protection.
Unable to match up against athletic rushers one-on-one.

strengths: Possesses a rare blend of quickness and explosiveness.
Plays with knock-back pop and ability to battle double-teams.
Slide quickness to elude blocks in short spaces.
Flashes big-time burst to run down the line and capture ball-carriers.
Acceleration inside the pocket to close out against quarterbacks.
weaknesses: Short arms fall below the NFL standard.
Play is too upright and lacks proper knee/hip bend.
Unable to consistently shed blocks with timing.
Struggles to create vertical rush openings on his own.
Needs to improve hand usage and craft go-to rush moves.

strengths: Adequate slide quickness and range in his pass sets.
Can unlock his hips and scramble into recovery mode if needed.
Inside hand jabs opponent’s inside shoulder in early stages of the rush.
Average athlete but solid in handling move-blocking duties.
Decent job of striking with hand leverage into kick-out blocks on the edge.
weaknesses: Below-average upper-body development and play strength.
Inconsistent punch timing allows rushers into his frame.
Waist-bender with too much “catch-and-ride” in his protection.
Sits tall in his stance and stays tall at the point of attack.
Needs to fit blocks with more force and finish the block at a higher rate.

strengths: Ran for 1,315 yards and 12 touchdowns with Cal in 2023.
Does a good job of setting up blocks in counter game.
Runs with adequate vision and lane choice.
Keeps feet moving as he processes moving fronts.
Has some speed-building once he gets into space.
Adequate pass-catcher coming out of the backfield.
weaknesses: Tight-hipped and lacks short-area elusiveness.
Missing initial burst through the hole and around the corner.
Averaged 3.3 yards per carry over his final two seasons.
Doesn’t run with enough violence when finishing carries.
Lacks commitment in pass-protection duties.

strengths: Slides laterally, mirroring receiver’s release from press.
Backpedals with good bend and balance.
Balances positioning against high-low route combos.
Good change-of-direction quickness on the move.
Makes good use of ball-tracking in deep zones.
Closes to the C-gap and centers his tackles near the line.
weaknesses: Not enough size or length to play outside as a pro.
Below-average reaction time to play development.
Gets stuck in neutral before finding the accelerator.
Below-average recovery speed when beaten.
Lacks ideal aggression and tackle strength in run support.

strengths: Very tall with an expansive catch radius.
Averaged a touchdown every four catches in college.
Capable of making spectacular one-handed grabs.
Has access to a stride-and-separate button.
Good awareness to drop and drag both feet in-bounds.
Size/athleticism to add more yards with wiggle and force.
Showed rare speed/explosiveness at the NFL Scouting Combine.
weaknesses: Needs to make the drive phase more believable on comebacks.
Lacks experience beyond a basic set of routes.
Fails to stack and control cornerbacks on verticals.
Inconsistent use of size to battle for catch-space.
Waits on 50/50 throws to get there instead of attacking them.
Average catch focus and suddenness with his hands.

strengths: Brings heavy hands and powerful upper body to the party.
Uses brute force to knock blocker off balance.
Can stack and split double-teams when technique is dialed in.
Battles and fights back against down blocks.
Quick lateral response to tackle ball-carriers.
weaknesses: Plodding with below-average first-step quickness.
Tight hips create tall pad level and stiffer movements.
Hand usage and block take-on are still underdeveloped.
Pass rush is slow to develop and unimaginative.
Will be a 27-year-old rookie.

strengths: Athleticism would go from minus to plus with conversion from WR to TE.
Very long arms with a plus catch radius.
Displays an ability to drop and dig out low throws.
Reliable “big” as a zone-beater underneath.
Works back to the ball on short throws.
weaknesses: Made just 19 catches over the last two seasons.
Lacks hip sink to quickly slow and break.
Doesn’t have much feel for setting up routes.
Not enough separation to work down the field against man coverage.
Below-average wiring as a run blocker.

strengths: Quick transition from catch to run on short throws.
Hands are generally reliable as a possession target.
Good effort and willingness as a blocker near the line.
Uses inside hands and a balanced base to sustain on linebackers.
weaknesses: Routes feel unfocused and lack salesmanship.
Lacks burst on turns to uncover and open windows.
Short arms limit catch radius and ability to block longer edges.
Inconsistent centering up contact as a lead blocker between the tackles.

strengths: Broad frame with long wingspan.
Explodes into initial contact with impressive pop.
Drive power to move bodies around when he’s centered up.
Nasty finisher who relishes opportunities to dump defenders on the ground.
Possess size/strength to hold his ground against powerful opponents.
Maintains good base width throughout his pass set.
Ankle flexion allows him to sink a sudden anchor when needed.
weaknesses: Will overstep angle blocks, leading to imbalance when fitting up.
Plays with wide, inaccurate hands that limit sustain and consistency.
Effectiveness takes a sharp dip if asked to make blocks in space.
Slow reaction time when challenged by gaming rush fronts.
Lacks foot quickness to slide and mirror to both gaps.
Punch placement will need improvement.

strengths: Former triple- and long-jumper with explosive lowers.
Posted 16 passes defensed, including six interceptions, from 2023 to 2024.
Patient in the early phases, allowing receivers to declare before opening.
Quick, confident feet can match release and recover when beaten.
Oily hips create fluid movement and change of direction in space.
Has the size to become more imposing underneath.
Shows ability to gather and redirect when matching comebacks.
Doesn’t guard grass and will look to overlap in zone coverages.
Engulfs wideouts at catch point for fast finishes or pass breakups.
weaknesses: Struggles to stay in phase against speedy route benders.
Loses poise and will grab when the rep becomes challenging.
Needs better physicality to suffocate work space on verticals.
Displays some indecision on zone switch-offs.
More drag-down tackler than full-body hitter.
Missed 2025 season with ACL tear.

strengths: Rarely sits back and waits for the play to come to him.
Excellent pad level, base strength and contact balance.
Explodes heavy hands into climbers to separate.
Innate GPS finds the best paths to ball-carriers.
Rare to find a broken tackle against him on tape.
Closes distance and bodies through tackles with consistent wrap.
Sifts through route development and finds the nearest threat.
weaknesses: Made just 11 starts in his collegiate career.
Downhill drift loses pursuit angles when run bounces outside.
Needs to keep outside shoulder uncovered to maintain contain.
Below-average length and speed limits tackles chances wide.
Inconsistent feel for working under or over climbing blocks.
Slightly below-average cover talent in man and zone.

strengths: Has good size and 44 career starts.
Compiled 48:6 TD-to-INT ratio in three seasons at UConn.
Confident and decisive on controlled, quick throws.
Displays good timing delivering to his first read.
Able to read the full menu of progressions if needed.
Ready to uncork when plant foot lands on three-step drop.
weaknesses: Feet start getting antsy when he senses pressure.
More likely to break platform than climb or slide.
Below-average arm talent and drive velocity.
Poor ball placement forces open receivers to break stride.
Struggles to find range on deep sideline throws.
Below-average accuracy and execution when play extends.

strengths: Good foot quickness getting in and out of breaks.
Fights through physical coverage at the top of his routes.
Hangs on tight to the catch through heavy collisions.
Competitive running after the catch.
Fits up most base blocks with good body control.
Adequate radar to find work as a lead blocker across the formation.
weaknesses: Benefitted from schemed looks and busted coverages.
Has trouble reigniting his route quickly after slowing his feet.
Diminishing catch threat as the route travels vertical.
Struggles to hold the line against power at the point.
Run blocking needs better patience and consistency of technique.

strengths: Possesses a compact, durable frame to absorb heavy contact.
Kept multiple mouths fed with fairly even ball distribution.
Played with calm and confidence against Georgia’s defense.
Adequate processing and operational quickness.
Improved at allowing route combinations to mature before acting.
Uses a quick climb-step to generate momentum on drive throws.
Able to climb the pocket and deliver on the move.
Feathers in catchable deep throws against single coverage.
weaknesses: Operated in a very quarterback-friendly offense at Tennessee.
Inconsistent anticipation and timing when throwing outside the numbers.
Fastballs underneath diminish catch success and accuracy.
Will throw off-platform instead of sliding to make a level-footing throw.
Delivers a high number of off-frame throws to open targets.
Throws sky balls deep, allowing safeties time to range over the top.
Below-average escapability once pressure breaches protection.

strengths: Explosive athletic traits.
Capable of aligning in multiple spots around the formation.
Potential to attack the seams on vertical routes.
Good run-after-catch with ability to finish behind his pads.
Displays accurate radar to find and tag targets as a move blocker in space.
weaknesses: Routes lack focus, technique and consistent separation.
Needs to do a better job of fighting back against handsy coverage.
Below-average catch radius and hands as a pass-catcher.
Passive into contact on split-zone blocks versus edge defenders.
Inconsistent finisher’s mentality when he’s sustaining a block.

strengths: Team captain with compact frame and sturdy base.
Lost only one fumble in four seasons.
Shakes are subtle but effective to elude tacklers in tight quarters.
Able to process and cut to a new lane after hitting it downhill.
Does a good job of setting up lead blocks inside and in space.
Gets to his protection duties and squares up blitzers.
weaknesses: Will slow feet into contact between the tackles.
Doesn’t press the line deep on inside or outside zone.
Below-average burst through the line or to the corner.
Needs to play faster in the early stages of the run as a pro.
Not as prolific a tackle-breaker as expected for his frame.

strengths: Big game in upset of Miami, with 11 catches for 136 yards.
Accurate in tracking and recalibrating downfield course.
Knack for gaining late positioning on jump balls and fades.
Impressive body control makes difficult adjustments look easy.
Hands-catcher with good extension and sudden hands.
Shows ability to become an impactful run blocker.
Combines early elusiveness and plus physicality in run-after-catch.
YAC threat on screens and hitches.
weaknesses: Lacks sudden feet to slip by press on outside release.
Lets foot off the gas too quickly during the drive phase.
Must prove he can run an expanded route tree.
Deep throws to him will be contested due to lack of separation speed.
Touchdown opportunities were dropped in the end zone.
Inconsistent finishing routes when he’s not the primary read.

strengths: Displayed rare blend of quickness, explosiveness and power at pro day.
Plays with consistent effort on every snap.
Good conversion of weight-room power into functional football strength.
Uses active hands to improve his positioning against run blocks.
Quick first step creates opportunities to beat guards around the edge.
Agility/burst to track and close on QBs/ball-carriers.
weaknesses: Needs to improve block take-on consistency.
Has shed strength but lack of length can slow the execution.
Needs to develop his hand-fighting to unlock more rush potential.
Short arms allow sacks/tackles to slip from his grasp on occasion.

strengths: Big wide receiver with exceptional play strength.
Bullies man coverage when it’s time to win catch positioning.
Has dibs on all fades and back-shoulder throws in the red zone.
Ball-tracking, adjustments and timing are all top flight.
Simply doesn’t drop throws that are clearly catchable.
Ball skills shown on tape translated to practice field at Senior Bowl.
Drags cornerbacks along for a ride after the catch.
weaknesses: Lacks early burst to quickly bypass NFL press.
Needs to do a better job of selling the drive phase.
Pops upright when getting into his breaks.
Inefficient footwork at the top of the route allows coverage closure.
Modest separation quickness getting out of breaks.
Long legs create sluggish stop/start quickness.

strengths: Alignment-versatile with the ability to compete as a “Y” or an H-back.
Squares up, sustains and seals blocks at the first and second levels.
Attacks in-line blocking assignments with effort and solid technique.
Locates targets quickly and accelerates into contact as a lead blocker.
Varies route tempo and is elusive in beating redirection in space.
Fluid route-runner with ability to maintain play speed out of turns.
Exploits the middle with soft hands and absorbs contact comfortably.
weaknesses: Lacks ideal size and length of a traditional “Y” on the NFL level.
Could use a little better feel for space when sinking into zones.
Struggles to haul in low throws and passes off his back hip.
Run after catch is straight-line and features no elusiveness.
Needs to keep his hands inside through block engagement.
Still learning when to cut blocks off or wash them down.

strengths: Good wingspan and upper-body strength.
Decent positional blocking to turn blocks out.
Lands down blocks and stays tight to his target.
Slides feet to the pressure and centers up rushers.
Passes and catches twisters at a desired rate.
weaknesses: Below-average leverage fitting up his run blocks.
Rarely creates much displacement as a drive blocker.
Lacks rhythm and technique working his combo blocks.
Can be slow to land firm inside hands with his punch.
Punch becomes more of a push, exposing him to counters.

strengths: Made 43 career starts.
Impressive frame and length for a guard.
Able to rattle pads when he lets his pass punch go.
Wide frame covers up gaps and helps anchor against power.
Usually gets hips into position for back-side seal.
Adequate hand placement into first contact.
Tennis background bolsters body control and footwork.
weaknesses: Covers but doesn’t control inside/outside linebackers.
Lack of bend/leverage inhibits drive-blocking success.
Lack of finish allows his man back into the play too often.
Below-average technique and displacement on double teams.
Inconsistent staying wide and centered to sustain.
Could struggle dealing with B-gap blitzers and gaming fronts.

strengths: Carries an NBA small forward wingspan.
Effective use of length to stay separated from blockers at the point.
Widens the edge effectively and spills runs to the perimeter.
Spins a wide tackle web to ensnare runners anywhere near his gap.
Races out of stance and upfield into his rush.
Inside jab step baits tackles into pausing their slide and opening the edge.
Long arms and strides create inside-move pressure opportunities.
Rushes with effort and targets the throwing arm to generate strip sacks.
weaknesses: Tight hips force him into a more limited, linear rush attack.
Unable to sink and trim the edge tightly at the top of the rush.
Can’t generate enough leverage or power for speed-to-power conversion.
Drops his eyes when attacking blocks and loses track of play design.
Needs better upper-body strength to press and control the edge.
Hands and feet lack harmonious coordination.

strengths: Showed rare athleticism in pro-day testing.
Displays outstanding flexion in his hips and ankles.
Looked fluid and quick in field drills.
Twitchy with sudden feet in short spaces.
weaknesses: Has never played organized football.
Will need to add more mass to play off the edge.

strengths: Bounces downhill and recalibrates based on block development.
Can beat a blocker’s edge with force or finesse.
Uncorks ferocious strikes on blocks with hands or shoulders.
Oily hips and long strides allow him to elude blocks and alter pursuit.
Times run blitzes to pierce gaps and disrupt the design.
Talented blitzer with a bag full of rush tricks.
Euro-steps single blocks for sudden wins as a blitzer.
Drops low and cinches legs tightly to finish.
weaknesses: High-cut frame with an unorthodox build.
Inconsistent positioning and constriction of run fits.
Had issues dealing with the size and power of Alabama.
Needs to keep head and eyes up on block take-ons.
Below-average zone exchanges in coverage.
Can be mismatched on backs and tight ends.

strengths: Has physical and athletic tools to quickly improve protection.
Pounces out of stance and gains ground quickly on pass slides.
Maintains proper base width throughout his reps.
Stays square and rarely opens outside hip prematurely.
Adequate lateral quickness for cutoffs on first and second level.
Rattles opponent’s pads on first contact in run game.
Able to consistently displace his down-block targets.
weaknesses: Needs to come off the ball and into blocks with better pad level.
Doesn’t bring feet with him and slides off sustain blocks.
Inconsistent to improve positioning after fitting run block.
Pass punch can be looping and late with subpar hand placement.
Open frame gets him pushed into the pocket by speed-to-power moves.
Lacks firm outside hand to widen edge speed around the arc.

strengths: Outstanding size, length, strength and explosiveness.
Like a weighted blanket on the wideout when he’s in tight.
Long arms shade and overwhelm the catch-point when he’s in position.
Uses size and physicality to run through receivers and jar throws loose.
Very sticky as an open-field tackler after the catch.
Plays with above-average toughness in run support.
Able to shed perimeter blocks and get to the ball-carrier.
weaknesses: Hip tightness creates clunky openings against press release.
Double moves and route fakes tend to manipulate him more than they should.
Below-average make-up speed when beaten deep.
Delayed in his plant-and-drive from the top of his backpedal.
Needs to get his head around sooner to find the football.

strengths: Makes very quick responses to play design.
Base strength allows him to play through contact and recover.
Adequate pursuit speed helps him run down the play.
Sudden acceleration and change of direction to blitz or spy.
Awareness to shade zone and athleticism to play man coverage.
Offers value on kick- and punt-cover teams.
weaknesses: Narrow frame falls below NFL standards at linebacker.
Gets pushed around when playing near the line.
Unable to step downhill and properly leverage his gaps.
Will guess and miss his run fit when plays flow off-tackle.
Needs more consistent eye balance in his short coverage.

strengths: Possesses good wingspan with huge hands.
Sturdy and strong with ability to catch through heavy contact.
Has the hips and feet to run routes on all three levels.
Route adjustments against coverage traffic come seamlessly.
Consistently scrambles to freedom when the pass play extends.
Good leaper with basketball body control to lean and adjust to the throw.
weaknesses: Wasn’t as splashy or sure-handed in 2025 compared to his 2024 tape.
Drifts on route turns, opening a door for defenders to undercut.
Has some trouble securing throws that are sent to his back shoulder/hip.
Ducks head and leans into his run-blocking fits.
Lacks consistent positioning, hand usage and strain as a blocker.

strengths: Sees past initial set of traffic to set up future cuts/moves.
Works well on shallow crossers and deep overs.
Able to snap off out-breaking cuts with suddenness.
Set school record with six KR/PR TDs (four punts, two kickoffs).
Averaged 26.8 yards per punt return on 21 tries in 2025.
First tackle attempt is often eluded or broken on punt returns.
weaknesses: Desire to bounce returns wide could catch up with him in the pros.
Lacks short-area agility as a ball-carrier near the line.
Pops up tall getting into his breaks.
Corners rise up over his head on jump balls.
Short arms and smaller hands require an accurate quarterback.

strengths: Foot stays heavy on the gas pedal when hitting the hole.
Physical into contact and usually falls forward for yards.
Loads lead shoulder to batter tacklers on second and third levels.
Strong hips allow him to pull through tacklers.
Keeps his feet chopping and churning.
Needs technique work but is willing in protection.
weaknesses: Running style is more segmented than fluid.
Lacks imagination and creativity between the tackles.
Drops eyes and charges forward instead of searching for cuts.
Very little pass-catching production at Indiana.

strengths: Proportional, well-built frame with good lean mass.
Firm on contact and churns his legs to generate movement.
Adequate lateral quickness to reach and secure.
Brings a block-finishing mentality to the field.
Identifies pre-snap movement creeping into the box.
Shows ability to redirect and mirror inside moves with quick feet.
Will change his set points and hand attack in pass pro.
Able to sit down and hold his ground against power.
weaknesses: Below-average length allows defenders into his frame first.
Struggles adjusting block to sudden movement.
Lacks rhythm and timing when hitting the second block on combos.
Has a tendency to rush pass slides and tempt over-sets.
Restricted slide-steps can cause his gate to open too early.
Shows signs of breakdown at the top of the rush versus speed.

strengths: Speedy and quick to chase the action sideline-to-sideline.
Triggers with good speed and often meets the action near the line.
Bend and base strength bolster contact balance when leveraging the gap.
Impressive agility and change of direction as an open-field tackler.
Blitzes with excellent set-up and timing to assault the pocket.
weaknesses: Subpar instincts, with a tendency to lose track of play development.
Below-average pop when taking on and stacking lead blocks.
Pursuit angles to the perimeter are often too steep and lead to misses.
Frequently eaten up by play-action that pulls him out of position.
Needs better awareness and quicker response time in zone.

strengths: Tremendous first-step quickness and short-area agility.
Possesses elite playmaking range in both phases.
Uses various approaches and angles to attack pass pro.
Shows bend and acceleration around protection edges.
Has a nose for getting to quarterbacks from inside the pocket.
Makes plays in the backfield when allowed to twist and stunt.
Capable of separating and resetting hands at the point.
weaknesses: Will see a massive step up in talent across from him.
Lacks necessary size for functional contact balance.
Struggles to unglue once linemen sink their hands in.
Unable to stay in front of NFL drive blockers.
Niche NFL fit, relying on both scheme and down/distance.

strengths: Versatility to play multiple safety positions.
Anticipates route breaks and readies feet to drive.
Squeezes short routes and constricts windows in zone.
Competes through the catch-point and challenges the receiver’s hands.
Slides with release and redirects with purpose.
Able to run the alley and adjust his track on the move.
Cleaned up technique and consistency as a tackler in 2025.
weaknesses: Backpedal is upright and tight.
Savvy route-runners manipulate him out of position.
Below-average fluidity and change of direction.
Takes a beat to transition and reaccelerate.
Slow triggering downhill to close on the run.
Inconsistent finding run fits near the line.

strengths: Tied the FBS record (25) for consecutive field goals made to start a season.
Made 12 of 13 field goals from 40+ yards in the last two seasons.
Consistent end-over-end ball-striker with good lift.
Appears unfazed by pressure kicks.
weaknesses: Attempted just two field goals (with one make) from 50+ yards in the last two seasons.
Doesn’t build much explosion into his kicks.
Might lack the deep-ball range needed from NFL kickers.
Will need to prove he can handle kickoff duties.

strengths: Well-built with a compact frame.
Posted elite speed and explosion testing at his pro day.
Has speed/quickness to track routes over three levels.
Plays with good bend and lateral transition quickness.
Plus change-of-direction quickness and re-acceleration talent.
Has speed and toughness to operate as a gunner.
weaknesses: Below-average wingspan to play the football.
Inconsistent in balancing vision from zone.
Lacks ideal route anticipation and recognition.
Will face-guard and lose track of the quarterback.
Forced into drag-down tackling by bigger runners.
Inconsistent coming to balance as an open-field tackler.

strengths: Core special-teams talent with standout potential.
Fearless on cover teams and capable blocker on kickoffs.
Stays dialed into backfield to pick up assignments.
Plays with quick recognition of misdirection.
Above-average stop-start quickness in space.
Face-first mentality bolsters effectiveness as a blitzer.
weaknesses: Only one season as a college starter.
Below-average size sees him knocked around in the box.
Drifts into traffic and loses back-side containment.
Needs to get stronger and improve both block slip and shed.
Limited foot quickness and reactive agility lead to missed tackles.
Step slow triggering out of zone drops.

strengths: Rare tackle/TFL production for interior defender.
Built with well-proportioned thickness.
Jolts blockers with his knock-back power.
Uses leverage to pierce gaps and disrupt play design.
Excellent run-game recognition from early in the snap.
Fights to maintain run fits against wide zone blocking schemes.
Plays around blocks with his eyes to locate and track ball-carriers.
Makes accurate shed choices to defend both gaps.
Very good change-of-direction quickness in small spaces.
weaknesses: More of an ooze rusher than a pocket collapser.
Doesn’t gain enough width on lateral slide steps to threaten edges as a rusher.
Displaced up to three yards by hip-to-hip blocks.
Plays with slow power, making it tougher to quickly shed.
Delayed in activating hands and getting them into blockers.

strengths: Has good size and runs with bend/balance through contact.
Processes and adjusts to moving fronts without losing his pace.
Allows blocks to develop and plays with good feel for when to cut.
Sees and exploits overflows with steep back-side cut angles.
Not a third-down back but can pick up a blitz here and there.
Excellent ball security over the last two years.
weaknesses: Below-average burst through the line and into the second level.
More one-move runner than creative cut-stacker on the inside.
Lateral cuts/movements lack twitch and suddenness.
Lacks speed to threaten defenses as an outside runner.
Tackled quickly on short catch-and-run tries underneath.
Unlikely to earn third-down or special-teams consideration.

strengths: Build is broad and solid with good lean mass on his frame.
Has strength/athleticism to play in all run-blocking schemes.
Leverages hand strikes and stays centered on the block.
Hand strength and upper-body power to maul if needed.
Climb is under control and ready for contact with linebackers.
Transitions from punch to grip for quicker rush control.
Improved hand usage helps firmness of his anchor.
Redirects weight to inside moves with adequate fluidity.
weaknesses: Below-average ground gain with his first three pass slides.
Static, two-hand punch is ripe for swipes and counters.
Needs to keep weight back and guard against reaching/leaning.
Higher pad level could be a concern if he moves inside.
Inconsistent footwork to improve positioning in run game.

strengths: Brings culture-changing mentality and leadership.
Posted 64 tackles during the 2025 season, including 13 for loss.
Plays with nimble feet and sudden change of direction in space.
Twitchy trigger-and-burst from a standstill.
Rushes in, wraps and finishes before yards are added after the catch.
Displays rare strength and intensity to fight off bigger blockers.
Lowers miss rate by closing tight before igniting tackle.
Blitzes off the edge with plenty of heat.
weaknesses: Is a step late in matching pattern breaks.
Inconsistent locating and responding to route development.
Transitions run tight when his leverage is beaten.
Urgency causes overflow when pursuing wide.
Can come in a little too hot as a run blitzer.

strengths: Compact build with good balance and strong lower half.
Practice battles with Jordyn Tyson sharpened his game.
No free rides given from his physical brand of press.
Keys quarterback closely while managing route threats.
Searches for certain routes like a hitter looking for a pitch in baseball.
Competes hard at the catch-point, racking up ball production.
Plays the throw like it’s intended for him when in position.
Bodies up and finishes tackles at a good rate.
weaknesses: Lacks length and top-end speed for vertical challenges.
Has issues losing positioning late, even when in-phase.
Flagged for 13 penalties in the last two seasons, per PFF.
Prone to chomping on double moves.
Short-stepper with average closing quickness from the top of his drop.
Needs improvement at slipping blocks in run support.

strengths: Long, rangy and versatile in assignment potential.
Can spy, cover or blitz as a third-down option.
Speed and athletic ability for man matchups across positions.
Able to knife inside the gap and disrupt run-play design.
Plays off the block with good quickness to tackle.
Posted 31.5 tackles for loss in his last four seasons.
weaknesses: Underdeveloped instincts delay his flow to the football.
Struggles to take on blocks and leverage his gap.
Still learning to trust his eyes and trigger with timing.
Slightly tardy in jumping and shadowing break points from man.
Needs to play with better route squeeze from zone.
Settles for catching and arm tackling instead of running through the ball-carrier.

strengths: Well-built with strong hips and excellent balance through contact.
Provides a tough catch-and-run option to break tackles and move chains.
Snatches the football with plus grip strength to secure through traffic.
Plays with good sideline awareness to dot his toes in-bounds.
Vast special teams experience at Alabama and has kick-return value.
weaknesses: Posted just four career TDs and finished a season with more than 135 receiving yards only once.
Tight hips create linear movements and rounded routes.
Below-average burst out of breaks and after cuts as a runner.
Slow making adjustments on deep throws.

strengths: Plays with natural instincts and vision as a ball-carrier.
Accelerates into traffic with fearlessness.
Able to change direction without slowing run tempo.
Runs tough and with adequate contact balance.
Drops pads and delivers forceful blows to second- and third-level tacklers.
Good success rate chopping down blitzers in protection.
weaknesses: One-speed runner lacking ideal burst through the line.
Doesn’t elude tacklers as effectively once he hits second level.
Inconsistent in pass pro decision-making.

strengths: Makes use of stacks and rubs in route tempo to uncover.
Builds and maintains leads on posts and over routes.
Adjusts quickly to ball flight and distance as a downfield tracker.
Has hands and toughness for quick-access routes inside.
Outplayed cornerbacks at Senior Bowl practices.
Adequate wiggle and effectiveness after the catch.
weaknesses: Below-average play strength through press contact.
Rounded in-breakers allow coverage to converge and contest.
Sits in place instead of working aggressively back to throws.

strengths: Twitchy take-off getting off the snap and into his upfield rush.
Change-of-direction quickness to eat up a two-way go in the rush game.
Bendy lower half allows him to sink and trim the edge at the top of the rush.
Displayed improvements in his rush approach in 2025.
Good pop in his hands to attack blockers when technique is right.
Can give an aggressive re-route to tight ends getting off the line.
Played “Buck” edge position, showing ability to rush or drop and cover in space.
Above-average pursuit quickness to run and tackle in space.
weaknesses: Has trouble getting off of pass punches that latch into his frame.
Needs to vary his rush tempo to reduce predictability.
Still developing rush counters to find more answers when stalled.
Inconsistent technique/discipline to set a sturdy edge.
Will start tackle tries from a distance instead of closing out the runner.
Below-average awareness and recognition as a run defender.

strengths: High-end football character and trains like a pro at all times.
Revved up his production in second season at ASU.
Hands are heavy when he throws them with purpose at blockers.
Drifts downhill to help squeeze the run lane.
Makes himself big and wide as a wrap-up tackler.
weaknesses: Poor speed leaves little margin for error in diagnosis.
Must improve at properly leveraging his run fits.
Lacks short-area quickness and reactive athleticism.
Slow to disengage from the block.
Can be exploited in the passing game.

strengths: Greets blockers with quick, well-placed hands.
Works hands and feet in harmony to play around blocks.
Good use of length to separate from blocks in both phases.
Smooth rush take-off with good burst to the top.
Targets outside hand with accurate chop to soften the edge.
Efficient in cornering and hugging the rush arc.
weaknesses: Lacks ideal size and length as an NFL edge defender.
Below-average power at the point of attack.
Could find himself in tough battles against in-line tight ends.
Not enough momentum for true speed challenges.
Corralled by long-limbed tackles who strike and land first.
Needs to add more moves and counters to his rush bag.

strengths: Explosive athlete with blazing straight-line speed for his position.
Can stem his release and burst up the seam.
Flashes signs of quality catch focus in traffic.
Explosive hips can create a jolt on the move.
Adequate hand placement and strain at the point.
Chip-blocks before his release are not for show.
Athletic family, including his father, who played in the NFL for eight years.
weaknesses: Would benefit from greater physicality inside the route.
Has room for better box-outs when contested.
Has a tendency to drift into the top of his route.
Sits heavy on hitch routes instead of working back when needed.
Below-average size and strength for blocking NFL defensive ends.
Needs to be under control upon arrival as a move blocker.

strengths: Very slippery target to get hands on when he’s attacking gaps.
Displays twitchy lateral scrap quickness to the ball.
Above-average burst in sideline-to-sideline pursuit.
Agility allows for trickier tackle finishes at challenging angles.
Has the suddenness and athleticism to work around blocks.
Stays low and wide as a wrap-up tackler in space.
weaknesses: Works under blocks and loses his pursuit advantage.
Below average in attacking and shedding lead blocks.
Big blockers tend to stick and stay on him.
Too much sitting back as a bystander in zone.
Inconsistent recognition when diagnosing play-action.

strengths: Posted 24 tackles for loss, six interceptions and 10 sacks in the past two seasons.
Very decisive in coverage and against the run.
Instincts and speed will carry him to a lot of plays.
Reads blocking keys and races downhill into position.
Uses a dip and shoulder turn to consistently slip blocks.
Sudden with his change of direction in pursuit.
Runs with receivers like a cornerback in man coverage.
Can burst and grab anything that is in his area.
weaknesses: Low win rate when taking on blocks near the line.
Unable to leverage and save his gap against lead blocks.
Can be drawn out of position by glances and misdirection.
Inconsistent angles to the throw leaves him under catch point.
Made a few too many mistakes reacting to RPOs in 2025.

strengths: Strong base and is always the low man into contact.
Plays with default ruggedness and resists blocks at the point.
Tremendous hip power to uproot blockers.
Excellent contact balance and is rarely on the ground.
Pops, resets and reloads hands to preserve contain positioning.
Appears to have enough strength to battle when playing inside.
Efficient footwork to tighten his track and slice inside on T/E twists.
Hands are active and aggressive to displace tackle’s punch.
weaknesses: Slow twitch with below-average get-off.
Ducks head into contact and can lose his sight line into the backfield.
Below-average lateral quickness limits his ability to widen pursuit.
Gradual upfield tempo rarely creates quick wins.
Tight-hipped rush lacks stride and burst to win at the turn.
Below-average counter quickness to find inside pathways.

strengths: Runs routes with good speed variance and leverage to create openings.
Attacks the seam and displays some separation at break points.
Outstanding football IQ shows up with situational adjustments to the coverage.
Rare to see him drop an uncontested throw.
Able to catch with toughness into oncoming hitters.
Good win percentage on high-point and back-shoulder throws.
Willing as in-line blocker and can be effective with second-level sustain.
weaknesses: Can be a little slow to sit down and open on stop routes underneath.
Allows defenders too much leeway to challenge his catch space.
Inefficient with wasted motion fitting up his run blocks.
Needs more consistent entry angles as a move blocker.
Suffered ACL tears in high school (2019) and college (2023).
Subpar speed and athletic testing.

strengths: Productive pass-catcher for the past two seasons.
Adequate in hitting lateral landmarks on back side cut-offs.
Drives hands into first contact with good pop.
Adjusts his route to elude traffic and redirection.
Runs with aggression after the catch.
weaknesses: Ordinary speed, quickness and explosion testing at pro day.
Needs to do a better job of shielding defenders from the catch-point.
Takes time with change of direction inside his routes.
Must add strength to handle in-line blocking tasks.
Below-average strain and finish at the point of attack.

strengths: Has experience playing in a variety of linebacker alignments.
Displays solid pre- and post-snap recognition against the run.
Reads and trusts his keys to avoid misdirection.
Plays with heavy hands attacking move blocks
Adequate range in zone and physical at the catch point.
Has two older brothers with NFL experience.
weaknesses: Straight-liner lacking fluid change of direction in space.
Below-average block-slipper with average block take-on success.
Overflows the junction point during run scrapes.
Suffered coverage busts in the red zone in 2025.
Below-average reactive agility brings higher missed tackle total.

strengths: Upper-body strength to quickly press and shed.
Difficult for O-linemen to find leverage to displace him with single blocks.
Above average playing around blocks and into gaps.
Stays square and corkscrews post leg into ground to anchor.
Effective use of space to separate from blockers as a rusher.
Good lateral quickness as a looper in rush games.
Difficult to redirect him once he hits an edge.
weaknesses: Stubby frame with a lack of length that falls below NFL norms.
Will get engulfed by a properly fit double-team block.
Small margin for error in the first phase against run blocks.
Needs to improve his hand work as a pro pass rusher.

strengths: Made improvements across the board in 2025.
Has foot quickness to stay sticky underneath.
Feet are sudden and twitchy in space.
Triggers instantly from pedal or flat-footed.
Long arms allow him to wrap into the catch point.
Can punch, extend and discard perimeter blocks.
Above-average kickoff coverage and gunner talent.
weaknesses: Did not intercept a pass in his four seasons.
Instincts and awareness are below average.
A step late crowding receiver on posts and digs.
Needs to more consistently read quarterback's eyes and drops.
Plays the wide receiver instead of the route or the football.
Does a poor job of locating the football in man.
Needs tighter squeeze and spacing from zone.

strengths: Runs with decisiveness and a sneaky initial burst.
Able to plant and go at sharp angles on outside-zone runs.
Drops pads and finishes through contact with good balance.
Consistently cashes in on short “gotta have it” runs.
Willing and able in his blitz pickup.
weaknesses: Below-average top-end speed to create explosive runs.
Lacks elusiveness and change-of-direction quickness.
Rarely makes downhill linebackers miss in the hole.
Missed games due to injury in 2024 (knee) and 2025 (ankle).
Has been a non-factor as a pass-catcher.

strengths: Broad chest with a 6-foot-9 wingspan.
Fires off snaps with low pads and into leveraged contact.
Displays adequate balance and recovery through double teams.
Slides with lateral blocks and can make a stand on the move.
Changes directions pretty well in small spaces.
Twitchy upper body creates violent sheds against centers.
Dominated as a bull rusher at Senior Bowl.
weaknesses: Too reliant on size over technique when taking on double teams.
Lacks feel and efficiency in taking on down blocks.
Limited to early downs and two-gapping schemes.
Lacks initial quickness to create early wins as a rusher.

strengths: Team captain with steady catch production in his last three seasons.
Smooth in his transitions on vertical routes.
Consistent to make the tough catch when challenged.
Tracks the ball and makes adjustments like a pro.
Good feel for utilizing space properly against zone coverage.
Uses sudden, strong hands to quickly pluck and tuck.
weaknesses: Sluggish takeoff could doom him against NFL press corners.
Unable to back cornerbacks off with the threat of his long speed.
Lacks route burst to unstick from sticky man coverage.
Fails to hit a noticeable second gear to run under deep throws.

strengths: Plays with bouncy feet and a full-throttle motor.
Builds out his production with secondary effort.
Balanced base features good power transfer from hips to hands.
Good technique to punch and slip past edge blockers.
Eyes stay dialed into runners and play development.
Pursues laterally with good burst, leverage and angles.
Athleticism, quickness and hustle will fit twist-heavy units.
weaknesses: Misdirection takes advantage of his urgency.
Will need to lock into scheme assignment as a pro.
Short-stepping rush lacks vertical push to threaten the edge.
Fails to generate enough bull-rush momentum to roll tackles downhill.
Suffered ACL tears in 2022 and 2023.

strengths: Linchpin for Joe Moore Award-winning offensive line in 2025.
Good initial quickness with repeatable footwork on each snap.
Hits zone-blocking landmarks to take the lead in first phase.
Hand placement and leverage come straight from teaching tape.
Diligent in executing his first block before climbing on combos.
Races feet through down blocks to help uproot the opposition.
Protection punch is direct and lands with adequate pop.
Identifies second-level blitzers and reacts quickly to catch them.
weaknesses: Below-average physical traits and will turn 25 in October of his rookie year.
Short arms limit ability to seal and sustain in tight spaces.
Can be lifted and controlled by a stout nose tackle with quick hands.
Struggles to dig deep with his rush anchor.
Lack of length limits recovery range from gap to gap.

strengths: Moves feet and resets hands to maintain block engagement.
Has a gritty finishing mentality that O-line coaches will appreciate.
Above-average feel for the proper angle to kick out and wall off his gap.
Adjusts in space as a move blocker much better than I expected.
Latches into defender’s pads/frame with strong hands.
Studious eyes in protection and searches for games/blitzes.
weaknesses: Plays with elevated pad level and lacks pop on contact.
Will have a tough time getting to lateral cut-off blocks.
Can be tossed off-balance by two-gapping opponents.
Leaning to anchor in pass pro opens him to counters.
Has problems sliding and redirecting pressure on his edges.

strengths: Comes from NFL bloodlines.
Improved technique and consistency in 2025.
Displays adequate field awareness.
Smooth pass-and-receive action in short zone.
Productive kick and punt coverage in 2024.
weaknesses: Still figuring out how to get to the catch-point.
Lacks instincts and route anticipation to make plays.
Inconsistent to diagnose and drive quickly from depth.
Rarely gets head around to find downfield throws.
Needs quicker breakdown to tackle when pursuing wide throws.

strengths: Disciplined in reading keys and being in the right place at the right time.
Plays with above-average ball skills and instincts from depth.
Tracks and adjusts to ball flight like a center fielder.
Closes on receivers with accurate angles to stop them cold.
Comes to balance, meeting runners with a wide base.
Explodes hips into ball-carriers for real stopping power.
weaknesses: Below-average arm length and wingspan.
Trigger quickness and burst from depth are ordinary.
Athleticism and speed appear to be severely lacking when playing in space.
Lacks reactive quickness and agility as open-field tackler.
Pursuit angles deteriorate when the chase stretches out.
Will enter camp as a 25-year-old rookie.

strengths: Three-year starter and team captain.
Has plenty of experience in pro passing concepts.
Patient to wait out the route development.
Throws with adequate timing into intermediate windows.
Good eye manipulation to move zone defenders away from his target.
Keeps eyes alive and searching for a completion when scrambling.
weaknesses: Below-average size, arm talent and drive velocity.
Low arm slot makes him susceptible to batted passes.
Exaggerated pre-throw ball pat slows his operation time.
Can be a step slow to hit open receivers in stride.
Accuracy issues appear tied to a lack of discipline with footwork.
Inconsistent poise in the face of real or simulated pressure looks.

strengths: Operates with consistent snap velocity.
Placement is generally consistent on long snaps.
Had to peel off and make challenging blocks in punt game.
weaknesses: Had some issues with throwing it low and inside on short snaps in 2025.

strengths: Elite blend of size, strength and high-point talent.
Once he gets top position, it’s too late for the defender.
Dominant win rate on contested catches during career.
Displayed better vertical push on comeback routes in 2025.
Catches with sudden, sticky hands outside his frame.
Able to leap, adjust and extend to make circus catches.
Defenders struggle to get him down quickly after the catch.
Has the ability to connect and dominate as a run blocker.
weaknesses: Delayed release creates early traffic against press.
Speed is more build-up than run-by.
Unable to shake clingy coverage with route running.
Route tree will come with fewer branches.
Lacks foot quickness to add yards after short throws.
Below-average scrambling to uncover on extended plays.

strengths: Decorated 100- and 200-meter sprinter in high school.
Five of his six touchdowns catches in 2025 went for 40+ yards.
Climbs past press coverage very quickly in vertical leads.
Tracks, gathers, elevates, adjusts and finishes downfield.
Extends his length to lasso throws from way outside his frame.
Erases safety’s post-catch tackle angles on digs and deep overs.
Flashes toughness and consistency catching through heavy contact.
Able to protect throws with body-catching into the traffic.
weaknesses: Needs to improve hand-fighting and learn to tempo his routes.
Gives early indicators of incoming route breaks to corners.
Inconsistent leveraging landing space on deep sideline throws.
Corners out-muscle him at the top of the routes.
Drifts into comeback routes instead of sinking and snapping it off.
Indecisive and lacking commitment on short catch-and-run throws.

strengths: Maintains field vision on every level of coverage.
Above average in reading and matching the route.
Decisive reading and driving from depth.
Makes well-timed leaps to attack high-points and break up throws.
Fluid laterally and changes directions with no hitch.
Good positioning to spill runs against the boundary.
weaknesses: Slender frame could require a move inside.
Can get outmuscled from press and inside the pattern.
Catching at the top of the route will lead to penalties.
Lack of tackle strength shows up in open field at times.

strengths: Long, stiff punches can disrupt release timing.
Catch radius and hand quickness fuel ball production.
Uses active, well-timed hands to attack catch-points.
Adequate transition burst out of his pedal.
Length helps him trap outside releases to the boundary.
Consistently gains position, then turns to locate the football.
weaknesses: His backpedal is choppy and tall in space.
Linear frame with tight hips that slow transitions.
Will lose ground and allow separation to speed.
Excess grabbing/pushing leads to high flag count.
Does not appear very interested in mixing it up in run support.
Loses containment, allowing runners to outflank him.

strengths: Of his six touchdown catches over the past two seasons, five went for 30+ yards.
Able to drop his hips to shut it down at the top of the route.
Locates the football and alters to a winning position on deep throws.
Able to make acrobatic in-air adjustments to make the catch.
Made some winning blocks on touchdown runs.
Showed great speed and explosiveness at pro day.
weaknesses: Only one season of notable production over four years at Georgia Southern.
Plays with average competitiveness and play strength.
Long strides into turns slow his momentum.
Average use of his size in shielding cornerbacks from the catch point.
Catch focus is average.

strengths: Unique size and a massive wingspan.
Made noticeable strides from 2024 to 2025.
Uproots and moves 3-techniques when running feet on double teams.
Uses mass to cover and obstruct on base blocks.
Does a nice job of maintaining length advantage with his timing.
Collects and controls rush with a strong grip.
Rare for rushers to crowd into his frame.
weaknesses: Speedy edge benders will be tough for him to match at the turn.
Needs a firmer, more proactive punch instead of catching in pass pro.
Struggles to properly mirror inside moves and spin counters.
High center of gravity creates a constant leverage disadvantage.
Lurches past his base, creating imbalance as a run blocker.
Lacks fluidity and first-step quickness to hit second-level landmarks.
Below-average agility and timing climbing off combos to the second block.

strengths: Voted team captain for 2025.
Creates leverage points to strike from.
Accelerates through contact and runs his feet.
Strong hands allow for longer sustains once he’s in.
Efficient eluding traffic and keeping routes on time.
Adjusted catch-timing to the barrage of fastballs he saw.
weaknesses: Relatively new to the game, making just 12 college starts.
Light production includes one career touchdown.
Long-legged and gradual through route turns.
Must become more forceful at clearing out catch space.
Hands fly out wide into contact on too many run blocks.
Forward lean diminishes mirror effectiveness in pass pro.

strengths: Comes from NFL bloodlines with a father who played in the NFL for 10 seasons.
Sets up workable spin counter at the top of the rush.
Adequate closing speed when chasing quarterbacks.
Lands hands first and sets an adequate edge.
Plays off blocks when he’s ready to pursue or tackle.
weaknesses: Below-average pad level and leverage at the point.
Needs to play with better strength and aggression versus the run.
One-speed rush rarely creates uncertainty for tackles.
Tightness prevents desired bend-and-flatten at the edge.
Lack of short-area agility and entry accuracy leads to missed tackles.

strengths: Has big, strong hands and a compact frame.
Strong at the point of attack with above-average contact balance.
Latches in with his hands and stays connected to the block.
Hand placement, leverage and powerful hips help him clear run gaps.
Keeps his inside hand in leveraged position during protection.
Absorbs bull rushers without giving much ground.
weaknesses: Below-average foot quickness and athleticism.
Lacks snap-to-step quickness in the run game.
Players with length can separate and earn quick wins against him at the point.
Below-average slide quickness to seal gaps in pass pro.
Mirroring loopers after pass-offs will be challenging.
Tight hips limit his ability to open and recover.

strengths: Upfield movements are highly efficient in both phases.
Inside spin at pocket depth finds pressures.
Hands and feet to hit well-timed inside rush moves.
Quick wins at the point with footwork or shock-and-slip move.
Twitchy and strong when he wants to shed the block.
Relentless in his pursuit of the ball-carrier.
Uses big hands and good length to disengage when captured.
weaknesses: Short-strider who needs more steps getting to the corner.
Not equipped for consistent speed-to-power conversions.
Rush hands are active but lack displacement power.
Knocked around when he ends up inside the box.
Strong in-line tight ends can neutralize him at times.
Gets widened off his spot by kick-out blocks.

strengths: Very consistent during three-year run as a starter.
Hand placement and leg drive make it easier to clear out gaps.
Takes good angles to linebackers and adjusts to their movement.
Pushed the Michigan front around with his drive blocking.
Base width and posture stay steady through protection rep.
Dense and firm on contact to set solid anchors against power.
Powerful shoves redirect blitzers off their track.
weaknesses: Short arms create smaller margins for error near his edges.
Must stay very tight to his block to maintain engagement.
Average lateral range to make play-side cut-off blocks.
Gets overextended when setting too heavy on his outside foot.
Chases the picker too deep against twist games.

strengths: Threatens vertically with build-up speed as seam-runner.
Maximizes catch radius with good extension and catch timing.
Adequate contested-catch talent with good feel for boundaries.
Top speed makes him hard to catch in the open field.
Determined and gritty as a point-of-attack blocker.
Good patience and radar to land blocks on second level.
Core special-teams talent is part of his profile.
weaknesses: Has trouble slipping around route redirection.
Tip-toe strides limit ability to quickly get in and out of breaks.
Below-average burst and separation from change of direction.
Struggles to elude the first tackler after catches underneath.
Inconsistent hand placement leads to uneven block sustain.
Needs to cut back on the leaning and lunging into block fits.

strengths: Two-time team captain.
Viable safeguard with his pass pro in the backfield.
Stalks and centers run block, exploding hands into contact.
Good balance as a run blocker with a wide base and active feet.
Attempts to make finishing his block a top priority.
Typical fullback toughness after the catch.
weaknesses: Lack of length can turn him into a one-pop blocker.
Hands land high, diminishing ability to sustain blocks.
Average adjustments to moving targets on second level.
Not a “clean-up man” as a lead blocker in the hole.
Average hands and relative non-factor as a pass-catcher at Michigan.

strengths: Loose-limbed and athletic with a full route tree available to him.
Speed to climb past unsuspecting safeties on vertical shots.
Able to adjust routes at a high rate of speed.
Separation burst across the field creates chunk-play chances.
Aligns wide to mismatch defensive backs on back-shoulder throws.
Rarely leaves yards on the field with his run-after-catch.
Athletic and fluid working from block to block in space.
Improved his body control and fit accuracy on lead pulls.
weaknesses: Not strong enough for adequate block sustain at the point.
Hand placement as a run blocker needs significant work.
Not a natural pass plucker and fights certain throws.
Needs to identify throws and move faster in making adjustments.
Needs to prioritize ball security on his run-after-catch.

strengths: Plays into gaps with decisive forward charge.
A regular in the backfield, with 32% of his career tackles resulting in a loss.
Uses active hands to work off of move blocks.
Well-timed long-arm strike creates space for his rush.
Two-hand swipe is quick and strong.
Sets up his helicopter-like inside spin.
weaknesses: Run out of his gap by forceful drive-blockers.
Doesn’t transfer much power from hips to hands.
Average change-of-direction quickness in space.
Below-average ground gain in his first three rush steps.
Speed-to-power conversion is underwhelming.
Not a natural bender, causing inconsistent contact balance.

strengths: Freaky weight-room strength shows up on the field.
Athletic for his size with ability to adjust in space.
Uses size and nimble feet to climb and cover linebackers.
Stones the rush when landing hands directly into frame.
Uses grip strength to cinch up rushers who crowd him.
Keeps his pads over his shoes when passing off twists.
weaknesses: Pops upright after snap and stays that way throughout the play.
Use of outside hands limit ability to control and sustain.
Inconsistent generating explosive contact at the point.
Base narrows when mirroring the rush.
Deadens feet on contact, allowing some leakage into gaps.

strengths: Was a 40-game starter over four collegiate seasons.
Big, broad frame with a 7-foot-1 wingspan.
Able to latch into rushers with big, strong hands.
Has the mass and power to create early movement.
Adequate job of neutralizing base blocks in run game.
weaknesses: Lacks fluidity from snap movements into block fits.
Below-average body control to sustain his run blocks.
Heavy feet in his diagonal and vertical pass slides.
Struggles with redirecting against inside moves.
Needs to play with firmer hands in pass protection.

strengths: Loose hips and good short-area foot quickness.
Runs well and displays easy change of direction.
Good feel for route combinations in zone.
Combative and competitive at the catch point.
Consistently locates the football down the field.
Stays balanced and tackle-ready in run support.
weaknesses: Gets overpowered by big receivers in press coverage.
Grabs and makes unnecessary contact downfield.
Very average at finding angles for pass breakups.
Can be dragged a little as a tackler in the run game.
Will be a 25-year-old rookie.

strengths: Good combination of size, athletic testing and production.
First with his hands and keeps pad level low through engagement.
Displays adequate play strength to shed and tackle.
Plays through redirection at the top of the arc when cornering.
Hands stay active throughout the rush rep.
weaknesses: Needs better first-step recognition against lateral blocks.
Didn’t face NFL-caliber tests against his 2025 competition.
Doesn’t always play with the quickness he shows in testing.
Squared-off upfield rush is easy to find for a tackle's punch.
Below-average counter quickness and rush instincts.

strengths: Gets back end of the defense lined up and is a strong communicator.
Shows good awareness to play design and his assignments.
Responsive to what his eyes are telling him.
Descends on crossing routes with timing to strike or quickly tackle.
Posted seven interceptions over the last three seasons.
Above-average ball skills when he’s in position.
Productive special-teams player throughout his career.
weaknesses: Needs more weight on his frame for demands of the position.
Can be baited shallow and beaten high on levels concepts.
Displays below-average man-cover talent.
Lacks top-end speed to drive and range off the hash.
Below-average reactive agility as an open-field tackler.
Made troubling mistakes as a run defender late in the season.

strengths: Rare arm length, hand size and wingspan for a tight end.
Will run past linebackers and beat them up the seam.
Has the sink and footwork of a big wideout getting out of breaks.
Impressive timing, body control and extension to big-boy defenders at the high-point.
Makes highlight-reel grabs look easy at times.
Has become a living nightmare for man defenders in the red zone.
Ricochets off contact to keep run-after-catch going.
Able to wall off and stay connected as a blocker in the slot.
weaknesses: Gets jostled off course by route redirection.
Lacks salesmanship to move defenders with leverage and head fakes.
Can get lazy at the top of his route, allowing easier close outs.
Allows defenders to blitz his work space and jar catches loose.
Doesn’t fit his lead blocks with good pad level or purpose.
Wild hands fly outside the frame when fitting up move blocks.

strengths: Good athlete with versatility to throw, catch, run and return kicks.
Instinctive and natural navigating traffic as a route-runner.
Good change of direction and balance with the ball in his hands.
Hands are natural and typically reliable when throws are on target.
Gadget-play ability opens a new slate of misdirection for creative play-callers.
weaknesses: Average re-acceleration to elude tacklers after making a cut.
Needs to do a better job of disguising impending route breaks.
Short wingspan requires optimal accuracy/timing from his quarterback.
Lacks north/south juice as a kick returner.

strengths: Posted 17.5 sacks and 33.5 tackles for loss from 2023 to 2024.
Footwork off the snap is highly efficient and effective.
Subtle hands and upper-body turn help him slip around blocks.
Delivers violent, stinging chops to tackle’s outside hand as a rusher.
Developed a workable spin counter.
Steady through his base to play through contact.
weaknesses: Lacks NFL size and length along the edge.
Build-up rusher who lacks explosiveness in tight spaces.
Below-average burst to threaten the edge.
Needs to improve hands for better block take-ons.
Average bend limits his anchor and change-of-direction quickness.

strengths: Blends imposing size with impressive straight-line speed.
Agile feet and loose hips allow for smooth change of direction.
Has burst to keep the run wide and beat flowing pursuit to the corner.
Reads blocks and moves fluidly through lane development inside.
Size allows him to run through cornerbacks in run support.
Able to drive through tacklers and fall forward for additional yardage.
Can do damage with big plays on swing passes.
weaknesses: Needs to gather steps getting into downhill cuts in outside-zone schemes.
Can be a step slow to see the opening and trigger downhill.
Needs to become more willful and aggressive between the tackles.
Lacks sudden acceleration for ideal escapability in small spaces.
Fumbles have been an issue over the last two seasons.
Has size for pass protection but is missing consistent grit.

strengths: Doesn’t shy away from run-blocking duties.
Keeps feet chopping through contact for longer connection.
Adjusts his track to land with some pop as a move blocker.
Runs a more expansive route tree than most tight ends.
Snaps off breaks with crisp cuts at the top of the route.
Enough speed to stress linebackers on seams and overs.
weaknesses: Inconsistent centering block fits near the line.
Stays busy as base blocker, but the strain is average.
Needs to keep his hands inside opponent’s frame.
Acceleration out of breaks is below average.
Drops and double-catches showed up in 2025.
Needs more toughness boxing out on contested targets.

strengths: Possesses NFL-caliber size and length.
Adequate get-off and timing to attack the turn.
Gives inside and outside fakes to help set up his rush.
Effective inside move fueled by pad level and efficient footwork.
Uses length to separate from and discard blockers.
weaknesses: Below-average weaponization of hands with his pass rush.
Average bend makes him easier to redirect up the arc.
Needs more strength to improve edge-setting and block take-ons.
Lack of explosiveness in pro-day testing was a bit concerning.

strengths: Rare blend of size and speed.
Play strength to disrupt releases and route timing through force.
Works cleanly through early route combinations in short zone.
Speed allows him to recover from missteps.
Range and length to bring down runners/receivers quickly in space.
Has ability to become a more impactful/productive run defender.
weaknesses: Made just eight starts in four seasons at BYU.
Below-average technique and discipline in press-man.
Struggles with indecisiveness in his read/reaction from off-man.
Below-average fluidity in change of direction when shadowing routes.
Needs his feet under him to prevent imbalance when planting and driving.
No interceptions in 36 career games at BYU.

strengths: Quick get-off and presses forward with his rush.
Accurate, well-timed hand swipes eliminate blocker’s punch.
Able to bend and flatten at the corner or slip inside the tackle.
Impressive efficiency and harmony between hands and feet.
Has speed for play-side and back-side pursuits.
Athletic with short-area footwork to finish tackles.
Flashed cover talent in his limited reps in space.
weaknesses: Only one season as a full-time player at the FBS level.
Will need to implement an inside counter at the apex of his rush.
Can be smothered by length against jump-sets.
Plays under the block and loses contain positioning.
Poor hand placement and block take-on technique.
Struggles to punch, extend and post up as an edge-setter.

strengths: Split low with impressive balance through contact.
Football smart and quick to diagnose blocking schemes/play design.
Highly efficient block-beater in tight quarters.
Will punch and work around or under blocks.
Sheds blocks with good timing to make the tackle.
Meticulous tackler with accurate aim and proficient wrap-up.
Former safety with good eyes and awareness in zone.
weaknesses: Loses ground when pursuit spills wide.
Feet are a little heavy when moving laterally.
Below-average reactive agility to mirror runner’s cuts.
Might need to play more proactively than reactively as a pro.
Below-average change of direction and range from zone.
Average man-coverage talent against NFL targets.

strengths: Flashes quickness to uncover against man underneath.
Sure-handed with the ability to make sudden adjustments on the throw.
Survives and thrives when faced with punishing catches.
Dips and drives into first contact from his hips.
Delivers first strike with inside hands into the defender’s frame.
Releases and scoops linebackers as a play-side zone blocker.
Finishes blocks and runs after the catch with the same energy.
weaknesses: Light production levels as a pass catcher.
Can do a better job of working back on the throw.
Inconsistent footwork on his double-teams with tackles.
Needs to come to balance more quickly as a blocker in space.
Room for improvement with his block sustain on second level.

strengths: Posted 17 tackles for loss, including 12 sacks, in his impressive 2025 season.
Sets up a softer edge with an inside/out rush approach.
Operates with violent chops and swipes at the top of the rush.
Good ankle flexion allows him to squeeze the rush arc.
Low-cut and difficult to knock off-balance.
Back-side closing burst helps to halt runs before they start.
weaknesses: Below-average size and length.
Lacks pro-level juice to beat most NFL tackles to the corner.
Square rusher lacking elusiveness and upper-body turn.
Gets engulfed and wired to blocking tight ends in run game.
Lacks length to help save contain positioning on wide runs.

strengths: Enticing blend of size, speed and overall explosiveness.
Easy acceleration to gain advantages on outside runs.
Tremendous power in his hips to drag tacklers with him.
Dangerous pass-catching option on angle and wheel routes.
Understands protection duties against the blitz.
Good mirror and hand usage to keep rusher in front of him.
Full-time kick returner over his first three seasons.
weaknesses: Below-average vision and feel for lane development.
Relies on very clear points of entry to make his mark.
Struggles to gather his weight and cut quickly on wide runs.
Despite speed, had few explosive carries in 2025.
Lacks elusiveness and runs to contact far too often.

strengths: Broad through his chest and waist with a burly lower half.
Built for creating barriers to run lanes inside.
Presses blocker into an extended, ineffective position as two-gapper.
Has the take-on pop to dissuade the double team.
Drives through sealed blocks, caving them into the gap.
Makes it difficult for blockers to sustain and finish against him.
weaknesses: Average hand quickness getting into first strike.
Eats blocks but lacks agility to make plays beyond his gap.
Below-average change-of-direction quickness.
Upright and straight-legged as a pass rusher.
Lacks short-area athleticism to make tackles in his gaps with consistency.

strengths: Quick jump-cut with ability to escape sudden foot traffic.
Runs with good bend and leverage.
Plays behind his pads and accelerates through contact.
Rarely bounces it unnecessarily wide or off track.
Experienced as a kick returner over the last two years.
weaknesses: Plays at a single tempo with limited speed change.
Average acceleration for outside runs.
Cut transitions are segmented and labored.
Too many drops and double-catches out of backfield.
Will turn 25 years old before camp begins.

strengths: Good arm length with big hands.
Plays with punch-ready hands and adequate scan for games.
Decent rush control when his punch locks in.
Plus quickness getting to back-side and play-side blocks.
Works from block to block with good timing and rhythm.
weaknesses: Below-average reactive agility and overall footwork in run game.
Reaches and lunges at second-level targets on climbs.
Lacks ideal play strength to mitigate his taller pad level.
Can lose base width during pass slides and turns into a heel-clicker.
Needs to become more consistent with his punch approach and timing.

strengths: Pro frame with very long arms.
Comes off the snap intent on keeping pads low.
Times up long-arm move to separate in early stages of the rush.
Attacks tackle’s outside hand with long-arm chop to open the edge.
Reads pocket climb and works back under to harass quarterbacks.
weaknesses: Rushes and corners beyond pocket depth too often.
Below-average get-off with a pedestrian closing burst.
Inside moves and counters lack suddenness.
Needs to better develop his core and upper-body strength.
Sloppy with his stack-and-shed technique.

strengths: Good forward lean and early acceleration into his routes.
Short-area quickness with the ability to uncover on outs, stop routes and crossers.
Pure speed to beat linebackers matched on him in man coverage.
Catches with soft hands and adequate extension to meet the throw.
Uses choppy settle steps and good hand strikes into base blocks.
Creates leverage with proper bend and lift technique.
weaknesses: Short arms lead to challenges rescuing off-frame throws.
Inconsistent finishing catches through heavy contact.
Average elusiveness to add yards after the catch.
Needs to get stronger in his upper body for blocking duties.
Caught leaning too far forward when getting into blocks.

strengths: Tenacity and toughness allow him to punch above his weight class.
Elite quickness to reach, scoop, cutoff, climb and pull with success.
Keeps defenders occupied with consistent foot drive through contact.
Always active and engaged with a commitment to blocking schemes.
Fits up blocks with a feel for angles to leverage the run lane.
Meets pass rushers with first hands on most reps.
Hand resets and lower-body flexion allow for quicker anchors.
weaknesses: Smallish frame falls below NFL standards.
Lacks mass to bring much pop into first contact.
Out-leveraged and pulled off his spot by big nose tackles.
Struggles playing with width to protect both A-gaps.
Bull rushers can build downhill momentum against him.

strengths: Dips hips and drives through down-block targets.
Keeps hands tight, providing added control at the point.
Stays square with eyes to the linebacker on work-up blocks.
Slides hips into place to secure on ace blocks.
Uses independent hands with good timing and pop.
Gets to resets quickly to help regain his positioning.
Base width never changes when mirroring.
weaknesses: Could struggle finding landmarks in outside zone.
Below-average second-level adjustments.
Can be steered and shed by longer nose tackles.
Forward-leaning pass sets invite counters.
Below average cut-off quickness when recovering.
Anchor gives way against powerful bull rushers.

strengths: Long limbs with a frame capable of adding more muscle.
Discards base blocks with sharp punch-and-pull technique.
Length to leap and tackle from distance on back-side chase.
Adequate take-off in his race to the corner.
Utilizes chop-and-rip move to trim the edge.
Presses up on tackle before hitting smooth inside counters.
Effectively disguises an inside spin counter.
weaknesses: Hip tightness turns him into a linear rusher.
Too many similar attacks down the middle of the tackle.
Unable to bend enough to avoid redirection past the arc.
Doesn’t generate enough leverage in his speed-to-power.
Below-average play recognition and in-line anchor.
Takes shoddy angles into contact as a tackler.

strengths: Has vision and athleticism to play high safety or in the slot.
Elite instincts help to feed his greedy mindset against offenses.
Plays with ideal decisiveness in both man and zone looks.
Anticipates route breaks and dashes into the passing lane.
Possesses ball skills and sticky hands of a receiver.
Adjusts his pursuit angles at full speed while running the alley.
Tackles receiver quickly to stop any run after catch.
weaknesses: Lacks top-end speed and could struggle with vertical matchups in man.
Undercuts routes at steep angles, leaving him susceptible to chunk plays.
Slender frame shows up in run support.
Inconsistent to close space properly before breaking down to tackle.
Becomes a block magnet when perimeter blocks land.
Comes in way too hot as an open-field tackler, leading to misses.
Lacked speed and explosiveness in pro-day testing.

strengths: Plays bigger and stronger at the point than his size would indicate.
Press strength to separate from tackles on contact.
Adequate ability to set an edge and anchor his ground.
Uses spin counter to free himself from blocks.
Hip bend and hard-driving feet create momentum at the corner.
Sequences an outside Euro-step with a chop-rip move.
Upper-body turn allows him to flip hips past tackles.
weaknesses: Undersized and lacking typical length for stand-up edge.
Inconsistent matching early lateral steps to secure contain.
Gets swallowed by big bodies when trapped in the box.
Below-average playing through contact.
Average get-off fails to stress tackles.
Long tackles can sideline his rush plans.

strengths: High-octane rusher with explosive take-off up the field.
First three steps to the top of the rush are fast and furious.
Has the bend and ankle flexion to dip under taller tackles.
Will surprise tackles with his ability to convert speed to power.
Carves out a more shallow path into the pocket against plodding tackles.
