Darts across blocker’s face and into gaps with excellent pad level.
Able to chase and capture running backs with exceptional pursuit speed.
Recovers from awkward angles to tackle.
weaknesses: Gets hung up on base blocks due to lack of length.
Has trouble maintaining his hand stack and holding his ground.
Was engulfed and pushed around in Oklahoma’s loss to Texas.
Long tackles are able to widen out his entry points on vertical rushes.
Hand fighting could use more polish at the junction point.
Missed multiple games in 2022, 2023 and 2025.

strengths: Uses leverage to draw defenders out of his route path.
Snaps off sharp cuts on in-breaking routes.
Able to make athletic in-air adjustments to his back shoulder.
Effective use of catch radius to snare throws outside his frame.
Should get some speed back with another year removed from ACL surgery.
weaknesses: Slow getting off the line and on top of off-man coverage.
Lacks acceleration to generate early vertical separation.
Doesn’t appear to run many routes at his true play speed.
Won’t make many tacklers miss after the catch.
Lacks technique and tenacity to block in-line on the pro level.

strengths: Smooth runner with the bend to cut sharply and force misses.
Doesn’t need to be cleanly blocked up to make it happen.
Displays impressive vision, instincts and creativity.
Changes tempo and pathways, making it hard to square him up.
Fights through contact with a well-balanced base.
Drops and drives through his pads when it’s time to finish.
Natural pass catcher capable of adjusting to the throw.
weaknesses: Patient but needs to be quicker in anticipating lane development as a pro.
Pulls zone runs off the track before giving the line a good pressing.
Lacks breakaway speed to finish runs from distance.
No special-teams play in his college football background.
Will be a 26-year-old rookie.

strengths: Uses length and accurate hands to stay separated from blockers.
Two-gaps with lockout and gets his head around to find the runner.
Possesses the strength and length to quickly shed blocks.
Competent arm-over move to slip past guards and into gaps.
Works from blocker to blocker with active hands.
Adequate lateral foot quickness to challenge edge as a rusher.
weaknesses: Average mass and only one season as a full-time starter.
Doesn’t capture the neutral zone with snap quickness.
Allows pads to rise high on lateral movements.
Has trouble finding his anchor against double teams.
Plays with inconsistent body control on the move.

strengths: Jarring production includes 39.5 tackles for loss in the past three seasons.
Set NCAA record for forced fumbles (17) with intent and technique.
Plays with heaviness in his hands and pads as a striker.
Able to absorb blocks with balance and hold his ground.
Can work around a block or greet it with a smack.
Pursues with leverage and is a successful tackle-finisher when he’s in position.
weaknesses: Hip tightness restricts his agility and lateral quickness.
Below-average reaction time and flow to stay ahead of blocks.
Missing desired sideline-to-sideline speed to close and capture to the perimeter.
Slow to gather his weight and change direction in the open field.
Suffers from coverage confusion and loses track of targets, leading to big plays.

strengths: Light on his feet with the ability to skip past press.
Runs routes with impressive short-area footwork.
Good awareness of spacing against zone.
Works aggressively back to the throw.
Makes well-timed adjustments late in the catch phase.
Hands are sticky and do their job to finish through contact.
Possesses slip-and-go talent to add yards after short catches.
weaknesses: Fairly easy to find and redirect for defenders playing short zone.
Lacks threatening upfield burst in his drive phase.
Struggles to bypass and quickly stack off-man corners on verticals.
Below-average hip sink for clean in-and-out at break points.
Routes are often too rounded, creating drift upfield.

strengths: Unusually fast/explosive athletic testing for an interior defender.
Initial quickness creates penetration opportunities for slanting fronts.
Has burst to race down the line and squeeze the run lane.
Uses a clubbing right hand to open rush lanes.
Effort and athleticism help generate his secondary rush.
weaknesses: Disappointing sack production relative to his athletic gifts.
Lacks functional anchor to fight off double-teams.
Needs to get better at using hands to stay separated from blocks.
Linear and too upright as a pass rusher.
Unable to generate an above-average bull-rush charge.

strengths: Four-year starter with experience at both tackle spots.
Slides in protection with ready hands and flat back.
Recovers his hands into inside positioning when they’re swatted away.
Consistently looks for additional work as a run blocker.
Strikes into run block with leveraged hands that carry plus grip strength.
Does an adequate job of rolling hips into his block sustain.
weaknesses: Hands lack needed precision to make up for his lack of length.
Oversets to his outside foot and gets beaten back inside by the rush.
Gives up too much ground against early bull-rush charges.
Gets stacked and pulled off-balance by twitchy edge defenders.
Inconsistent hands as a run blocker, diminishing his control and sustain.
Stiff-kneed when climbing to second level cut-off blocks.

strengths: Fits up run blocks with good timing, accuracy and balance.
Gets hips unlocked and rolls into drive blocks.
Strains as a base blocker and works until the whistle.
Has frame/toughness to square off against edge defenders.
Impressive technique and core strength make him trustworthy in protection.
Catches with reliable hands and consistent focus.
Unfazed playing through route and catch contact.
weaknesses: Requires more instinctive hand resets as a run blocker.
Long edge rushers can stab and discard his protection.
Struggle to separate from man coverage.
Gives breaks away and lacks burst out of his turns.
Below-average run-after-catch quickness.

strengths: Rushed for 177 yards and two TDs against Texas A&M.
Gains good lateral ground with swift jump-cuts to escape trouble.
Bendy in ankles and hips to quickly cut at crisp angles.
Burst takes him to his top speed in a hurry.
Slippery lowers and short-area wiggle create missed tackles.
weaknesses: Lacks tempo and rhythm in getting to run actions.
Hurries runs and either misses the entry point or runs into blocker.
Doesn’t take much for him to pull runs off-track and bounce them wide.
Lacks instincts and feel for setting up tacklers.
Missing the power and running style to collide and fall forward.
The tape suggests he is not a third-down option.

strengths: Outstanding size with above-average athleticism.
Erases space downhill and fills the front like a linebacker.
Crashes weight into targets to immediately stop runs.
Explosive pedal-to-drive transitions in space.
Striking power to intimidate receivers over the middle.
Has a chance to star on punt- and kick-cover teams.
weaknesses: Needs to do a better job of playing off of blocks.
Long strider who loses pursuit leverage running the alley.
Rushes in hot and runs past entry points to tackle.
Gets late starts gaining depth from split-safety.
Below-average break anticipation in man coverage.
Responsible for red-zone coverage busts against Ole Miss and Tennessee.

strengths: Keeps runs on schedule and headed downhill.
One-cut quickness to snap it off at tight angles.
Shows ability to get skinny and sneak through tight crevices.
Accelerates through contact and consistently falls forward.
Tucks it tight and rarely puts the ball on the ground.
Reliable hands and catches when contested.
Shows a willingness to stand in and square up blitzers.
weaknesses: Inconsistent vision leads to missed opportunities.
Below-average burst through the hole.
Rarely seeks additional cuts once he makes a downhill decision.
Tight hips limit ability to open and stride in space.
Enters into contact with higher pad level.
Protection mistakes resulted in hard hits on the quarterback against Iowa.

strengths: Above-average wingspan with big hands.
Burst and stride length to eat cushion and blow by corners.
Settles and opens to quarterback quickly on zone-beaters.
Smart hand-fighting to swat away press and separate downfield.
Maintains top speed through vertical route transitions.
Elusive after the catch to shake the first tackler.
weaknesses: Upright into intermediate break points, requiring excessive gather steps.
Defensive backs can read/close on his curls and stop routes.
Below-average at bodying and shielding corners from catch point.
Needs earlier adjustments to underthrows for dibs on catch space.

strengths: Inside spin move can become an even more effective weapon.
Long-strider with the ability to quickly gain ground.
Rushes with consistent energy and urgency on each snap.
Very loose in his hips, allowing contortion around the block.
Uses punch-pull technique to get rid of base blocks.
Good back-side burst to close and tackle cutback runners.
weaknesses: Must add more muscle mass to hold up as a pro.
Lacks instincts and post-snap recognition.
Will struggle to hold the point and leverage gaps as a pro.
Missing base and contact balance to keep from getting jostled around.
Lacks the play strength to fight through redirect blocks.

strengths: Hits the ball with good consistency of force.
Led the FBS with 48.5 yards per punt in 2025.
Excellent at landing short-field punts inside the 10 over the last two seasons.
Adequate touch-to-toe time in his operation.
weaknesses: Poor hang-time numbers throughout his career.
Regularly outkicks his coverage.

strengths: Pass sets feature good posture and adequate balance.
Strong hands are quick and active in protection.
Mirrors rush movement with athletic basketball slides
Has the feet and hips to recover into position when needed.
Initial quickness to climb up and cut off back-side linebacker.
Drives hands into run blocks with proper placement/leverage.
weaknesses: Technique and instincts need a good deal of work.
Issues with footwork/body control to his landmarks as a run blocker.
Lacks proper timing on his work-up blocks to the linebacker.
Needs improved punch-timing to prevent being driven into the backfield.
Hasn’t learned to properly reset his hands when needed.
Struggled to redirect Missouri’s edge rushers up and around the pocket.

strengths: Impressive blend of athleticism, agility and core strength.
Gets into run fits with proper footwork and body control.
Excellent hand placement/resets to help control and guide the block.
Agile feet for smooth skip pulls around the corner and into space to lead the charge.
Uses strong hands and active feet to stay connected to his blocks.
Works well with teammates on double-teams and on twist pickups.
Pass protects with inside hands and a wide base to mirror.
Eyes work quickly to new targets when diagnosing rush games.
weaknesses: Doesn't have much knock-back pop into initial contact.
Can be extended and pulled off-balance by salty two-gappers.
Average snap-to-step quickness to slide laterally and slow A-gap shots.
Can be impatient climbing and stalking second-level linebackers.
Beat to the punch at times due to below-average arm length.
Has some trouble protecting his edges in pass pro.

strengths: Long line of collegiate/pro athletes in his family.
Relied on as an extension of the offensive line at Stanford.
Comes off the ball with good knee bend, pad level and inside hands.
Runs through initial contact to pop opponents and establish the tone.
Footwork and positioning to help seal the edge on wide runs.
Well-schooled in working up to the linebacker on combo blocks.
Gets in and out of breaks/stems with good efficiency.
Runs behind his pads after the catch and is tough to bring down.
weaknesses: Outside arm will reach and hook defender when he misses positioning.
Fails to disguise route intentions and elude man coverage.
Can be crossed up by throws that are outside his frame.
Needs to play bigger when contested.
Must do a better job of using size/strength to carve out catch space.
Holding penalties were an issue for him in 2024.

strengths: Excellent blend of size and top-end speed.
Showed tremendous leaping ability at his pro day.
Displays good change-of-direction quickness.
Adequate burst to challenge in a race to the corner.
Has size and strength to protect his quarterback.
weaknesses: Runs a little too tall through the line and into contact.
Average feel for run-lane development inside.
Reacts to linebackers instead of setting them up.
Has runs where he will tap breaks slightly through contact.
Below average as a pass catcher out of the backfield.

strengths: Good blend of height, weight and arm strength.
Release quickness and velocity to beat coverage into tight windows.
Trusts his offensive line to do its job in protection.
Adequate poise to stand and deliver in the face of pressure.
Can make all the throws and is decisive in attacking Cover 2 windows.
Adequate scrambler/runner with toughness to grab extra yards.
weaknesses: Below-average accuracy working outside the numbers.
Deep throws come out too flat and with inconsistent depth.
Completion percentage hurt by habit of rushing throws unnecessarily.
Rips gas at short and intermediate targets when touch is needed.
Struggles to find proper placement on anticipatory throws.
Needs to work on sliding and eliminating head-first dives.

strengths: Attacks lead blockers with good downhill force.
Quick to flow into run fits and squeeze the lane.
Adequate pursuit speed and short-area burst.
Gains depth quickly on spot drops in coverage.
Smooth in space to flow with quarterback’s eyes or cover man.
Praised by staff for his character.
Showed toughness, playing through a torn meniscus for most of 2024.
weaknesses: Instincts and recognition of blocking schemes are average.
Climbing blockers tend to stick to him once they land.
Below-average block-slipper in space.
Struggles to consistently square up tackle targets.
Excessive arm tackling leads to high miss count.

strengths: Quick to process the front and find lanes without slowing.
Patient with block development.
Knee-bender with good tempo in stacking cuts.
Good decisiveness whether running inside or outside.
Natural instincts for creating space from tacklers.
Battles and rejects tacklers, using base strength and balance.
Willing to square up in blitz pickup.
weaknesses: Needs to incorporate more urgency to hit the hole quicker.
Had just 13 of 165 carries go for 10+ yards in 2025, with a season long of just 20 yards, per PFF.
Five fumbles in the last two seasons.
Lacks focus and technique to be a third-down option.

strengths: Late comer to the sport with athleticism and passion to keep improving.
Gets off the snap and up the seam with enough speed to threaten.
Hands are soft and confident when attacking the high point.
Former goalkeeper with good body control.
Finds a second gear in the open field.
Fits up run blocks with balanced base and inside hands.
Good feel for leverage on lead pulls in space.
weaknesses: Upright and elongated getting through his routes.
Incoming break points are too easy for coverage to decipher.
Average acceleration to separate coming out of turns.
Slow to pluck and tuck the throw, leading to missed catches.
Too much coasting into the top of the route on zone-beaters.
Lacks necessary mass and grit for in-line blocking duties.
Takes inconsistent angles climbing to linebackers.

strengths: Very good at dipping under and around blocks.
Stays wide in the hole to meet the runner.
Explodes through contact when possible.
Twitchy trigger sends him racing to the action.
Impressive burst and pursuit speed across the field.
Starred on kick-cover teams early in his career.
weaknesses: Too much unnecessary movement during the diagnosis phase.
Uses short bursts instead of measured flow to the football.
Lacks instincts and feel for play development.
Gets stuck on blocks that get into him.
Inconsistent maintaining zone integrity.

strengths: Elite production, ranking among the FBS’ top three in catches, yards and touchdowns.
Rapid foot turnover quickly eats cornerback’s cushion.
Instant acceleration becomes runaway speed.
Sinks hips and bursts out of turns/breaks to open the throwing window.
Turns short slants into big plays after the catch.
Threatens all three levels of the field.
Good body control with sudden, late hands on contested sideline throws.
weaknesses: Wastes motion against press instead of winning clean.
Mechanical routes don’t always feature his true speed.
Body-catcher who loses focus over the middle.
Lacks extension and hand strength to finish when contested.
Fast post-catch but only average elusiveness.
Needs to make quicker adjustments to capture air space on 50/50 balls.

strengths: Balance and leverage to stay upright and in the play.
Typically beats lateral blocks to the spot.
First step and closing burst are both rush weapons.
Sets up edge attack with well-timed slide step.
Works two-hand swipe and slap/swim to clear the blocker’s edge.
Gets to instant counters when the initial rush stalls.
weaknesses: Smaller with shorter arms.
Engulfed once bigger linemen latch on.
Lacks length for two-gapping and mass to fight double teams.
Pressure production generated by the same move/approach.
Missing power element to collapse the pocket.
Season-ending ankle injury in October required surgery.

strengths: Makes good use of arm length in playing the ball.
Shut down TE Max Klare in 2024 in his man-cover reps.
Good reactive quickness to lateral break points from off-man.
Quick to read the run and get downhill to fit it.
Special-teams star during his college career.
weaknesses: Lack of speed limits range in coverage and in pursuit.
Loses ground when forced to flip and run with vertical routes.
Could use better eye balance between route and passer.
Struggles coming to balance and will overrun the tackle in space.
Inconsistent at centering and running through as a tackler.

strengths: Posted 11 sacks, 19.5 tackles for loss and 6 forced fumbles in 2024.
Possesses upper-body strength to press and stack tackles.
Builds a wall as edge-setter, turning runs back inside.
Effective use of hands to quickly get off blocks.
Long-arm rush can devastate tackles and roll them into the pocket.
Attacks and dislodges blocker's outside hand to open the corner.
Keys backfield to bring his rush with the quarterback's movements.
Drives and steers blockers out of the way inside the pocket.
weaknesses: Lacks firm pop into first contact versus run blocks.
Gets widened out when drive-blockers land first.
Below-average pursuit speed chasing from the back side.
Short-strider lacking burst in his upfield climb and cornering.
Static frame for tackles to find with their punch.
Slow to reset secondary effort when bull rush stalls.
Needs to diversify his rush tools and menu.

strengths: Posted 17.5 tackles for loss and 11 sacks in the last two seasons.
Good first-step quickness and burst off the snap.
Plus lateral movement down the line of scrimmage.
Above average use of hands in both phases.
Good cross-face pass rush success on the move.
weaknesses: Faces a huge step up in competition.
Doesn’t impose his will consistently enough.
Below average knock-back pop into first contact.
Will struggle to hold ground against angle pressure.

strengths: Low-cut with oily hips and twitchy feet.
Fast, agile feet in his mirror and transitions.
Balanced mover with sudden change-of-direction quickness.
Usually stays tight to route stems and turns from tight press-man.
Crowds receiver and closes his catch window when contesting downfield.
Urgency and physicality show up consistently at the catch point.
Quick close-and-tackle limits yards after catch.
Serious run supporter who usually defeats his blocker.
weaknesses: Has takeaway twitch but rarely seen challenging into passing lanes.
Needs quicker depth gain from zone relative to route concepts.
Face-guards from start to finish instead of locating the ball when his back turned.
Still has some technical issues to clean up in man.
Sometimes leans into receivers instead of using feet to close distance.

strengths: Very tall with an impressive wingspan and plus ball skills.
Able to knock releases off course with his length.
Will squat on and jump short routes when he senses it.
Impossible to throw over him when he’s in phase downfield.
Keys quarterback to squeeze routes from zone shuffle.
Gains speed quickly when his legs unfurl.
Expansive tackle net to bring in open-field runners.
weaknesses: Pops upright as his pedal progresses.
Hitch in his transition when break catches him off-guard.
Willing to grab instead of trusting technique and size.
Athletic but struggles guarding multi-breaking routes.
Inconsistent body control as a tackler.

strengths: Consistent production in all categories as a pass-catcher.
Tall target with impressive length and expansive catch radius.
Plays with plus catch focus to bring in contested throws.
Hands are soft and natural to gather throws.
Catches with hand extension away from his frame.
Able to swerve and elude early tackle tries, stacking yards after the catch.
weaknesses: Needs more muscle mass and general play strength.
Far too easy to reroute his release with force.
Monotonous route tempo makes it easier to squeeze his route.
Lacks suddenness and burst getting in and out of turns/breaks.
Slow to sink and open to his quarterback on stop routes.
Mentality is too passive as a potential NFL run blocker.

strengths: Compact with a thick base and good balance.
Plays the game with an abundance of energy and urgency.
Scrapes with pace similar to the run concept.
Stays low to dip and slip incoming blocks.
Closes quickly on ball-carriers in short spaces.
Accelerates through contact to deliver pop on tackles.
Diagnoses screens quickly and hustles to shut them down.
weaknesses: Needs more patience downhill to avoid losing gap leverage.
Undersized and gets knocked around by big-bodied blockers.
Struggles shedding blocks once they sink into him.
Short arms limit consistent wrap finishes as a tackler.
Has issues losing track of zone threats.

strengths: Rare size/athleticism for the position.
Provides dual-theat attack options and can work off script.
Tall with an elevated release point for layered throws between the numbers.
Does an admirable job of tying feet to eyes through progressions.
Gets through progressions smoothly when he finds his rhythm.
Throws with adequate velocity for tight-window throws.
Can outstride pursuit and turn scrambles into first downs.
Ability as a runner gives his offense a chance when passing is off.
weaknesses: Disappointing passing results when he breaks the pocket.
Processing and timing need improvements on the next level.
Struggles with post-snap recognition of zone lurkers underneath.
Needs to more consistently quiet his feet to improve accuracy.
Needs to get better at manipulating and holding coverage with his eyes.
Throws with a long-limbed delivery that slows his operation and release.
Aims throws that turn into misfires or underthrown interceptions.
Too willing to move backwards instead of climbing when under pressure.

strengths: Quick to shake press with his stutter-and-go release.
Above-average acceleration for a long-legged wideout.
Downhill speed features a second gear to run under deep throws.
Tracks and adjusts paths to deep throws with consistent focus.
Good footwork for quick in-and-outs at break points.
Hip sink to stop quickly on comebacks and zone-beaters.
Juice to eliminate pursuit angles with the ball in his hands.
weaknesses: Struggles to bring in tough catches despite his wingspan.
Inconsistent stacking cornerbacks on deep routes.
Doesn’t impose his size on catch points nearly enough.
Will face a huge step up in coverage quality in the pros.
Very minimal run-blocking on tape.

strengths: Above-average athlete with agile feet.
Smooth pedal mirroring release from press-man.
Anticipates and shadows the break points.
Transitions forward quickly from the top of his drop.
Faces up and finds the football when phasing vertical routes.
Size and length are disruptive when he’s contesting.
Runs through pass-catchers with jarring force.
weaknesses: Lacks consistent decisiveness and route balance in short zone.
Has trouble staying tight to the route from trail positioning.
Plays upright in off coverages, creating imbalance in transitions.
Missing the same energy he shows as a run tackler when striking receivers.

strengths: Carries a broad build with stocky limbs.
Productive 2024 season with 40 tackles (7.5 for loss) and 2.5 sacks.
Beats center into the neutral zone with quick hand strike.
Leverage and base strength to hang in against double-teams.
Works to maintain his run fit while moving laterally.
Adequate short-area quickness to tackle his gaps.
Powerful picker on T/E and T/T twists.
weaknesses: Short arms allow blockers to crowd his frame.
Takes time to free himself from connected blocks.
Below-average range getting down the line and stopping the run.
Takes blocks on but not enough block destruction.
Lacks ideal burst to create momentum for his bull rush.

strengths: Can punt, kickoff, hold and even step in for emergency field-goal tries.
Gets it from touch to toe with proper quickness.
Hits punts with pro-caliber hang time.
Adequate accuracy as a directional punter.
weaknesses: Hasn’t proven to be a field-flipper with his leg strength.
Lack of touch turns into touchbacks on short-field punts.
Nose-drop technique yields inconsistent results to deaden the punt.

strengths: Physical and aggressive from press.
Quick to crowd into receiver’s chest on deep routes.
Gets his head around in time to locate deep throws.
Plays with above-average discipline and feel in zone.
Above-average closing quickness from his drop.
Finds openings to snake his hand into the catch-point.
Smallish but comes downhill ready to help against the run.
weaknesses: Loses positioning to release fakes from press.
Average anticipation and transition quickness at break-points.
Lack of size/length limits on-ball production.
Allows too much room after the catch for add-on yardage.
Struggles to quickly bring down running backs.

strengths: Fires heavy-handed punch to fit and control run blocks.
Gets into first contact with surprising bend for a tall tackle.
Plays with finisher’s mindset when he gets chances to bury blocks.
Stays sticky to blocks with vice-grip hand strength.
Maximizes length with a firm, well-timed punch into rushers.
Unlocks hips and turns out ankles to sit and anchor against power.
Showed improvement in identifying end/tackle twist games in 2025.
weaknesses: Will struggle to reach landmarks for back-side cut-off blocks.
Lumbering movement limits success in space and on combo climbs.
Can be displaced by stout 4i/5-technique take-ons.
Labors out of his stance and is late to his set-points against speed.
Average ability to recover and redirect once his edge is compromised.
Still overcommits and rides too deep on some end/tackle twists.

strengths: Finished with 46 career starts between UTSA and Texas.
Highly productive across the impact categories.
Alters entry points and strings together smart hand attacks mid-rush.
Instinctive inside and outside spin counters await tackles.
Secondary rush is a consistent weapon for sack scooping.
Flashed competence and upside as an off-ball linebacker.
Adequate athleticism when asked to drop into space.
Pursues to the sideline with sound leverage.
weaknesses: Lacks a threatening burst to the top of the rush.
Hands are fast and active but lack sting.
Edge-setting and block-shedding run hot and cold.
Still developing timing and key recognition off the ball.
Average change-of-direction quickness when it’s needed.
Below-average pursuit speed and open-field fluidity.

strengths: Consistent ball-striker with good lift and placement.
Made 77% (10 of 13) of his kicks from 50+ yards out.
Capable as an emergency punter if needed.
Good size with ability to cover and tackle on kickoffs.
weaknesses: Missed 47-yard kick in overtime loss to Tennessee in 2024.
High percentage of misses were off the right hash.
Missed three field goals in the first game of 2025, including 39- and 40-yard tries.

strengths: Four-year starter and team captain in 2025.
Mirrors rusher while maintaining proper base width.
Has enough anchor for a move inside.
Hits hand resets with independent hands when needed.
Quick first step laterally creates advantages on reach blocks.
Keeps looking for someone to block until the whistle blows.
Fires stiff under-hooks and unlocks hips into his base blocks.
Displayed good radar and fitted contact as a lead-pulling center in limited snaps.
weaknesses: Average range in pass slides to patrol both A-gaps.
Lacks recovery agility when rushers beat him on his edge.
Below-average quickness and athleticism in space.
Two-gappers create early separation with length.
Leans into run blocks, creating imbalance and tougher adjustments.
Inconsistent technique and timing on his work-up blocks.

strengths: Team captain who sets the standard.
Work in the film room shows up in his pre-snap recognition.
Cleanly processes stacked release and route combinations.
Well-timed flip and run against vertical challenges.
Very good speed to stay in-phase or make up ground.
Excels with his ball-tracking and catch timing.
Accelerates quickly from any change of direction.
Excellent technique and toughness tackling near the line.
weaknesses: Plays tall and with a restricted backpedal from depth.
Feet have to do some of the work for his hips in coverage.
Needs to break down sooner in downhill pursuit.
Average lateral agility in coverage and as a tackler.
Entry points into tackles can run a little high.

strengths: Team captain with a good kick-slide.
Throws hands with adequate timing to maximize length.
Fluid change of direction to mirror inside moves.
Scrambles effectively with loose hips and quick feet.
Smooth on skip-pulls and athletic getting into space.
Hits lateral landmarks and is capable of working combos with timing.
weaknesses: Too easily knocked off his base at the point of attack.
Spends too much time on the ground.
Below-average grip strength and drive power.
Pushed and displaced by quality bull rush.
Needs to guard against lunging in pass pro.
History with ankle injuries could be a concern.

strengths: Hustles into coverage position on punts.
Punt snaps are generally on-frame.
Delivers short-kick snaps with good placement.
weaknesses: Below-average agility as an open-field tackler.
Long-snap speed falls below NFL mark.

strengths: Impressive athlete with good length and plus strength.
Versatility to play 3-technique or odd-front end.
Explosive first step makes him primed for penetration.
Can work quickly around blocks or press and separate to set his edge.
Twitchy in his upper half to shed blocks with force.
Rangy to chase and make down-line and open-field tackles.
Lateral quickness and agility is built for twists.
Spin counters come naturally with his rush.
weaknesses: Lacks typical lower-body mass for interior scraps.
Has to fight harder to hold off downhill run-blocking schemes.
Can be displaced vertically by stronger zone blockers.
Hasn’t incorporated much to set up his pass rush.
Needs better hand work and earlier counters to open rush lanes.

strengths: Offers end/tackle flexibility.
Good first-step quickness and pad level.
Uses the blocker's momentum to help work under the block.
Rushes with a consistent motor and good chase quickness.
Able to create lateral separation as an interior rusher.
Saw a bump in tackles for loss and sacks in 2025.
weaknesses: Started just five games in four seasons at Tennessee.
Hip stiffness limits his change-of-direction fluidity.
Blockers can get into his frame and engulf him at the point.
Below average stack-and-shed talent when taking on single blocks.
Doesn’t use hands to set up his rush often enough.

strengths: Three-year starter with major production splash in 2024.
Steadies into first contact with a strong, wide base.
Difficult for single blocks to generate movement against him.
Power to neutralize much bigger bodies when hands are first.
Pushes through edges as an interior rusher.
Has the leverage and leg drive to build a bull rush.
weaknesses: Lacks first-step quickness and upfield burst as rusher.
Tight lower half makes it tough to bend on the edges.
Short-strider with below-average burst to close.
Frame and game are caught between positions.
Inconsistent when digging in against down bocks.
Lack of agility manifests in missed tackles.

strengths: Good blend of size and length from the slot.
Sells his drive phases and disguises his break intentions.
Has footwork to open up his route tree underneath.
Former hooper with body control and timing to win jump balls.
Slippery to tackle once the ball is in his hands.
Played like he belonged against plus competition at Senior Bowl.
weaknesses: Needs to pick up the pace with his press release.
Possesses below-average burst and top-end speed.
Average re-acceleration to separate on whip routes.
Needs to become more efficient getting into breaks underneath.
Will be a 25-year-old rookie.

strengths: Rare combination of size and athletic testing results.
Carries almost 36-inch arms on a chiseled 300+ pound frame.
Explosive power in both upper and lower halves.
Displays twitchy, heavy hands getting through bag drills.
weaknesses: Has yet to play a game snap.
Rigid movements in on-field drills.
Needs to get used to moving with better knee/hip bend.

strengths: Size and length will be enticing for evaluators.
Offers versatility for alignments and assignments.
Quick to diagnose and close the distance to squeeze run lanes.
Flashes ability to drop pads and drive through runners/receivers.
Possesses anticipation and range to play over the top as a split safety.
Smothers downfield receivers in man coverage, tilting 50/50 balls his way.
Irritant, using length and well-timed swats to knock catch chances away.
Locked down Baylor TE Michael Trigg in their matchups.
weaknesses: Average transition quickness mirroring in-breaking routes.
Too passive in attacking and disrupting screen plays.
Doesn’t play with ideal urgency and toughness against the run.
Rides way too long on blockers instead of getting rid of them.
Slow to gather as an open-field tackler and overruns his mark.
Arm tackling allows runners to slip free from his grasp.

strengths: Long frame and long arms create advantages.
Posted 22.5 tackles for loss over the last two seasons.
Gains early separation to play around base and kick-out blocks.
Throws speed and angle changes into rush openers.
Good upper-body turn headed up to the corner.
Silky ghost move pairs with a long stride for quick pocket entry.
weaknesses: Roughed up and pushed around at the point.
Gets knocked off his base by average contact.
Average lateral agility and footwork in short spaces.
Average transition fluidity to his inside moves.
Inconsistent timing with his hand swipes.
Stiff punch and strong hands lock his rush up.

strengths: Rips off the line and into high gear on vertical routes.
Very good reacceleration on start-stop route breaks.
Has a history of securing catches through big hits.
Drops both feet in-bounds on most sideline throws.
Slips first tackle on a hitch and quickly gets upfield.
Took a kickoff to the house against Alabama in 2025.
weaknesses: Relatively sleepy catch production over four seasons.
Leans into breaks and turns, alerting the coverage.
Wasted footwork dooms comebacks and stop routes.
Inconsistent to track and adjust on deep throws.
Gives little help as a run blocker.

strengths: Adequate size with good length and big hands.
Dismisses eye candy and keys the back.
Scrapes with the runner’s pace along the front.
Gets his nose dirty when it’s time to fill and spill the run.
Played with better pursuit angles and control in 2025.
Reads/wrecks screens and shows good zone awareness.
Core special-teamer who thrives on cover teams.
weaknesses: Lacks base strength to balance and play through contact.
Rarely generates a clean win in his block take-ons.
Would benefit from lowering pads and slipping blocks.
Average agility and lateral quickness chasing runs wide.
Turns back to quarterback unnecessarily in some coverages.
Rarely runs through ball-carriers with stop-quick force.

strengths: Good read and response to blocking schemes.
Above-average straight-line burst to the perimeter.
Hammers incoming blocks with heavy lead shoulder.
Extra sauce as an open-field striker.
Good spatial awareness in zone coverages.
Makes it tough to layer throws over his extended arms.
weaknesses: Tight hips raise pad level and create linear movement.
Eyes drop to blocker instead of runner’s actions.
Leaves too much room for runners to operate when fitting the run.
Needs to be quicker in slipping blocks or playing off them.
Slow coming to balance and changing direction as a downhill tackler.
Below-average agility and change of direction as a tackler.

strengths: Dense frame with lower-body thickness and explosive hands.
Good transition quickness and stride length on inside move.
Constantly pushes forward, leading to secondary rush sacks.
Good knee bend to play under and through blocks off the snap.
Reads lateral block steps and quickly mirrors them.
Uses leverage and base strength to penetrate into the backfield.
Consistently wrecks collegiate tight ends at the point of attack.
weaknesses: Pass rush lacks diversity of approaches and attacks.
Upfield get-off unlikely to stress an NFL starting tackle.
A stiff punch will jostle and reset his rush when it finds him.
Overruns opportunities versus the run due to breakneck pace off the snap.
Inconsistent take-on technique to stack and separate from tackles.

strengths: Locks in on keys and dismisses eye candy in diagnosis.
Good straight-line speed in pursuit to the sideline.
Gets behind his pads and delivers strong contact.
Closes and tackles quickly on throws in front of him from zone.
Offers plug-and-play special teams value.
weaknesses: Average change-of-direction quickness and athleticism.
Works under blocks, allowing run lanes to widen.
Struggles to alter pursuit path to tackle in open-field pursuit.
Needs to drop his entry point to improve tackle success.
Below-average feel for managing space in coverage.

strengths: Able to kick off with loft and placement for pro consideration.
Made 88.9% of kicks in 2024, including 4 of 5 from 50+ yards.
Can deliver high, end-over-end makes when confidence is on.
weaknesses: Posted a career low in field goal accuracy rate in 2025 (76.7%).
Made just 2 of 7 field goals from 40-49 yards last season.
Struggled with ball striking and flight path in 2025.

strengths: Prototype measurables for a run-blocking “Y” tight end.
Rolls hips into base blocks and strains as long as he needs to.
Runs feet on contact to wash defenders away on down blocks.
Can help secure edges and climb on combos.
Plays with a lineman’s demeanor as a block-finisher.
Looked more comfortable in space in 2025.
Catches what is thrown to him.
weaknesses: Needs to lower pad level into first contact.
Hand placement can run too wide or too high.
Lacks fluidity and radar as a lead blocker.
Clunky feet getting off the line and into space.
Isn’t much of a threat with the ball in his hands.

strengths: Tall boundary cornerback with a wide wingspan.
Posted 24 pass breakups and four interceptions over three FBS seasons.
Played an even mix between zone and man at Texas A&M.
Good cut-off and physicality to lean on release from press.
Explosive hip flip to turn and sprint from his opening.
Drives from depth into the passing lane with compact strides.
Fights for inside leverage near the goal line when on an island.
weaknesses: Loses the race too quickly when taking on pure vertical speed.
Mental mistakes and early missteps turned into explosive plays.
Below-average route recognition and anticipation from zone.
Tall in his pedal, creating disjointed transitions.
Needs better fight getting off blocks and sticking his nose in against the run.
Way too much ankle-diving as an open-field tackler.

strengths: Posted six interceptions and 14 pass breakups since start of 2024 season.
Good physicality and squeeze in man coverage against tight ends.
Use of length makes it hard to complete touch passes over him.
Bursts downhill and accelerates into contact in run support.
Productive in coverage on special teams.
weaknesses: Slow to accelerate when forced to flip and sprint vertically.
Tight-hipped with below-average lateral transitions in coverage.
Uneven eye balance between route and quarterback from depth.
Struggles with functional change of direction as downhill run supporter.
Needs better wrap-and-run to finish his tackles.

strengths: Loose-limbed and athletic in his movements.
Adequate job of reading keys and tracking run-play development.
Possesses sideline-to-sideline speed in pursuit.
Can slip suddenly off the blocker and get in on a tackle.
Viable option as a blitzer and quarterback spy.
Ready to step in and compete for a role on kick-coverage teams.
weaknesses: Lacks ideal length and play strength inside the tackle box.
Struggles to take on and quickly shed blocks.
Bites way too hard on play-action bait.
Too many busts/missed coverage assignments on tape.
Fails to come to balance as a tackler, leading to a high miss count.

strengths: Well-proportioned frame with safety size.
Size, length and athleticism for alignment versatility.
Swoops down and gobbles up short throws in the alley.
Capable of matching up on both “Y” and “F” tight ends.
Pursuit range and length to erase mistakes up front.
Punt-cover talent drew the attention of opposing return units.
weaknesses: Play strength doesn’t match impressive measurables.
Below-average quickness to recognize play design.
Lack of coverage instincts limit catch disruption.
Loses leverage, allowing runners easy cut-backs.
Missing a consistent cinch-up when wrapping runners.
Lacks angle and tackle instincts in run support.

strengths: Stacked up pass-rush pressures despite modest number of reps.
Uses foot quickness for sudden entry-point changes as a rusher.
Attacks and gets rid of tight ends at the point of attack.
Takes shots from blockers but stays in the mix to make a play.
Lacks experience but has demeanor/traits for core special teams.
Showed impressive athleticism at his pro day.
weaknesses: Played in just 18 games, with 13 starts, at the FBS level.
Could struggle to set edges and quickly shed blockers.
Inconsistent grit taking on pulling guards.
Short arms allow tackles to consistently land the first punch.
Early indecisiveness limits finishes as a rusher.
Needs to develop a better rush plan and sequencing.

strengths: Top-end speed to pull away and take it to the house.
Praised for his work ethic and passion for the game at Georgia.
Above-average play strength to battle against hands-on coverage.
Able to stick and cut at full speed without losing his pace.
Can punish zone coverage when working between short and deep.
Piles on yards after catch when given too much breathing room.
Showed an ability to thrive through heavy catch contact.
Access to instant speed when he wants it.
Offers a dangerous punt-return option when teams need a play.
weaknesses: Large share of his targets were thrown behind the line of scrimmage at Georgia.
Developed a bad habit of jumping to make the catch when it’s unnecessary.
Rushes through his routes with little attention to detail.
Will need to eliminate his heavily rounded route turns.
Short arms will require an accurate passer.

strengths: More alert and decisive in coverage in 2025.
Versatility to play from a variety of alignments.
Drives downhill for physical finishes to plays.
Adequate feel for route development around him.
Rangy to the football from down or high safety.
Reads run and closes quickly, patching up leaky gaps.
Able to open hips and mirror runner’s lateral cuts.
Length is a catalyst for open-field tackling success.
weaknesses: Flows faster than change of direction can handle at times.
Needs to play with more consistent pad level in run support.
Must punch and play off blocks with better consistency.
Average stickiness to the route from nickel.
Average quickness to key and trigger from space.
Inconsistent locating routes that get behind him.

strengths: Twitchy off the snap with disruptive potential.
Much more difficult to block on the move in gaming fronts.
Can beat lateral zone blocks and find his way into the backfield.
Excellent awareness to recognize and chase screens.
Rare speed and short-area quickness for a defensive lineman.
Edge-to-edge rush ability when singled up on centers.
weaknesses: Smaller stature with short arms.
Put on skates and rolled out of gaps by down blocks.
Can’t play in schemes asking him to sit down and take on blocks.
Unable to withstand longer block engagement.
Lack of lever length and hand moves for early rush wins.
Doesn’t have enough brute strength to crank up a bull rush.

strengths: Builds his speed for adequate vertical push.
Late leverage pushes coverage out of his downfield break.
Sharp snap at the top of his comeback routes.
Legitimate ball-carrying talent on scripted touches.
Good vision, burst and wiggle to create for himself.
Return specialist with three career scores (two kickoffs, one punt).
weaknesses: Average early acceleration into his routes.
Short/intermediate routes are gradual and rounded.
Loses momentum in and out of slant stems.
Needs to stack and control corners when tracking.
Struggles to secure catches through contact.

strengths: Built to standard with size, musculature and limb length.
Transfers power from his hips to his hands on first contact.
Punches, extends and locks out when setting edges.
Attack-oriented with play strength for block destruction.
Powers through back-side cutoffs by TEs and maintains fits on lateral block engagements with OTs.
Well-suited for successful battles against “trey” blocks (OT/TE double-teams).
Good upfield pace to set up his speed-to-power conversion.
Adequate footwork and transition quickness on inside rush moves.
Drives through the edge with forward lean at the top of the rush.
Length, tackle strength and body control create good tackle finishes versus the run.
weaknesses: Could be a little quicker with his initial hand strikes.
Knee bend is average when leveraging the point of attack.
Pursuit burst and top-end speed are average.
Lacks a go-to move to soften the rush corner.
Struggles getting rid of tackle’s connected punch.
Length of Auburn’s Xavier Chaplin put his rush in neutral.
Rare to see a quality counter move when his rush stalls.

strengths: Massive with a rare combination of size, length and power.
Can align at multiple spots in even and odd fronts.
Uses length to create late separation from blocks.
Quick block recovery makes him tough to clear from his gap.
Long lateral stride helps him meet and thwart seal blocks.
Has more bull-rush upside than he currently taps into.
Generates knock-back power once he gets rolling downhill.
Blocked six kicks during his career.
weaknesses: High-cut build leads to waist-bending and elevated pad level.
Inconsistent body control and contact balance at the point.
Below-average block command and stack/shed on tape.
Footwork and agility fall below average.
Rushes with a narrow base and limited moves/counters.
Needs better hand placement to unlock his bull rush.
